ChIJmzWSur8EdkgRIoPuJsxp0pU.name: The Corner Café
ChIJmzWSur8EdkgRIoPuJsxp0pU.address: 179- UK, 182 Hercules Rd, London SE1 7JY, UK

reviews.text:
By the looks of most recent reviews, this place needs no introduction, but what’s another 5 star review eh?

Overwhelmingly deserving of the top honour, this very humble cafe serves delicious traditional breakfast, at honest prices, with huge portions. I was actually amazed when the plate hit my table.

The staff are lovely, and this cafe frequented by locals gives it a very grounded vibe. I’d love to claim that I have found a hidden gem, but it sits quietly in plain sight, noticed my many previous reviewers.

Cannot wait to return!

A very nice and friendly restaurant. The breakfast was delicious, everything was fresh and well prepared. The staff were welcoming and genuinely friendly, creating a pleasant atmosphere. Thank you for the tasty breakfast. We will definitely come back again.

The most quintessential English Breakfast I can imagine as an American tourist from NY. The care they give to every item is a joy to behold and to eat. What to get? Everything you can fit into your gullet.

ChIJKzfar20OdkgRC_aFViPKR54.name: London Buddhist Vihara
ChIJKzfar20OdkgRC_aFViPKR54.address: The Avenue, Chiswick, London W4 1UD, UK

reviews.text:
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ Europe’s Oldest Theravada Buddhist Temple – A Truly Peaceful Spiritual Home

London Buddhist Vihara is known as the oldest Theravada Buddhist temple in Europe, and it truly feels like a sacred and comforting spiritual home. I deeply love this temple because it brings peace, calmness, and healing to the mind the moment you enter.

The Chief monk ( Ven.Bogoda Seelavimala )is incredibly kind, compassionate, and deeply inspiring, offering wisdom, guidance, and warmth to everyone who visits. The vihara is beautifully maintained, spiritually uplifting, and filled with positive energy, making it a perfect place for meditation, prayer, and reflection.

Whether you are Buddhist or simply someone seeking peace, calmness, and clarity in life, I warmly invite everyone to visit this wonderful place. You will truly feel blessed to be here. 🙏❤️

⭐⭐⭐⭐⭐

I sent my wife there to check out this for the first time. She met with a monk. They had a good chat and she was asked whether she could buy some yarn of a certain colour from Singapore. And she did.

Quite easy to locate.
Originally built by Anagarika Dharmapala, it was the first ever Buddhist temple built on British soil. Housed inside a house it has that calming, tranquil feeling.

ChIJ_UZdjtsEdkgRcHnYGRr8sw0.name: St James's Café
ChIJ_UZdjtsEdkgRcHnYGRr8sw0.address: Horse Guards Rd, London SW1A 2BJ, UK

reviews.text: St. James’s Cafe in St. James’s Park is located in a beautiful area overlooking St. James’s Park Lake just step away from sites like Big Ben and Westminster Abbey. There’s plenty to choose from here for food and drink, including pre-packaged sandwiches, fresh made items from the deli kitchen and the sit-down menu. If you choose to sit at a table, order via QR code from there. When I was here it was pretty busy but my food arrived in a timely fashion. My Breakfast Bap and glass of wine were delicious. Seating is available outside during warmer weather. Very much recommended.

Overall I had a very nice experience here. The staff member who took my order was friendly and the hot chocolate was delicious.

While I briefly stepped away to use the restroom, my drink was cleared from the table. I had left my belongings there, but since there wasn’t much of the drink left, it may have been an understandable misunderstanding. It was just a little disappointing as I had hoped to relax and finish it.

Aside from that, everything else was great.

We visited St James's Café during a weekday lunch. Despite being busy, the café’s spacious layout made it easy to find a seat.

Orders were placed at the counter, and I chose the fish and chips paired with a hot chocolate. The food arrived promptly. The fish and chips were fresh and flavourful, while the hot chocolate was pleasantly balanced, not overly sweet, and suited my palate perfectly. Overall, it was a very satisfying meal.

The bankside café is the greatest place we’ve been to in London. Good food with great staff ! We paid 18£ for two full English breakfast and the plate were huuuuuuge. Thought that we had to add black pudding but the waitress told me that it was included ! Recommand this place more than others

The distance of this cafe was not very far from where we were staying. The food i got to say was brilliant. The cafe serves and caters to a lot more than just breakfast as you can have lunch and dinner here.

The staff welcomed you in which is always a nice touch they dont pressure you at all when placing orders. I have no complaints about this place the value for money was great.

The place was clean and tidy and i would 100% recommend this place.

Came to get the kids some non-sourdough toast, ended up ordering a cheese and ham roll which was lovely. Big lumps of cheese and nice ham. Delicious. The breakfasts being served were massive and plenty of people around enjoying them. Would return for good value food. Not high end but good honest grub nicely priced.

London Waterloo Station is one of the busiest and most important transport hubs in the UK, and it generally leaves a strong impression on both regular commuters and first-time visitors. The station is almost always busy, especially during peak hours, but it remains well organised considering the sheer volume of passengers passing through each day. Despite the crowds, movement through the concourse is usually smooth, with clear signage that makes it easy to find platforms, exits, and connections to the Underground or nearby attractions. One of the station’s biggest strengths is the constant presence of railway employees. Staff members are visible throughout the station and are usually approachable, helpful, and knowledgeable. Whether you need directions, platform information, or assistance with tickets, there always seems to be someone nearby ready to help and guide you in the right direction, which is reassuring in such a large and busy environment. Cleanliness is another positive aspect of London Waterloo Station. For a station that handles such heavy footfall every day, it is consistently clean and tidy. Platforms, walkways, and waiting areas are well maintained, and bins are regularly emptied, helping the station feel orderly rather than overwhelming.Overall, London Waterloo Station is a reliable, efficient, and well managed transport hub. While it can feel crowded at times, the helpful staff, cleanliness, and good organisation make it a generally positive experience for passengers.

Busy station that is not easy to navigate but not terribly difficult either, especially with the help of the friendly staff. As long as you position yourself in front of one of the main screens by the gates to the platforms you get informed in plenty of time to make it to your train along with the car you want. TIP: The graffiti tunnels are a great 10-minute detour if you arrive early and need to kill time.

Amazing architecture and the biggest Terminus station in London. Plenty of shops including Boots, TG Jones, M&S. Plenty of eateries on the concourse. Staff are always friendly and very helpful. The station is a five minute walk to the South Bank by the Thames. Many bus routes, a Sainsbury's and a lovely fish and chips shop called
The Fishcotheque. Situated just under the railway bridge just outside. IMAX Cinema is also situated just outside the station. Beautiful Station. Toilets are always clean and well looked after. Fully disabled access here too.

A cozy place, an hidden gem within the heart of East Acton. I keep revisiting this place for a hearty meal at least twice every week. It's become a favourite spot! The food is top notch! Creamy hot chocolate and wholesome pizzas.

We grabbed a meat feast pizza from this spot and it honestly hit the mark—tasty, loads of toppings, proper flavour. But the thing that stood out the most was the owner. The guy was genuinely nice, super chilled, and just had good energy. Out of nowhere he gave us free Cheez-It “just for fun,” which actually made the whole visit feel even better. Little moments like that make a place memorable. Good pizza, good people. Would happily go back.

Lovely spot and does just what you want from it. It's unpretentious, cheap and cheerful with friendly staff in an accessible location. Kid friendly too!

Pizzas are excellent quality for the price, have had them to eat in and take away as well. My daughter loves the Margherita pizzas, I tend to go for the pepperoni options, all very good quality at pocket friendly prices. Really recommend to any locals looking to support small independent businesses!

One of the best breakfasts I have ever eaten. The full English was excellent, elevated to a higher level than most with the use of seasonings, garlic and garnish. Every item in the packed skillet was of a high quality and cooked perfectly, I particularly loved the home made herby sausage patty. Pork belly on a breakfast was something I've never seen before but was juicy and succulent. Place was very clean and staff were friendly and efficient,   the owner even coming out to chat to everyone was a nice touch. If you want a top class breakfast that is above and beyond the average breakfast (and a great pot of tea) you must try this cafe ! Highly recommend. 10*

Fam went on a week vacation in London and found this gem, went here twice, met the amazing owner and enjoyed authentic english breakfast 😋

All dishes we tried were cooked fresh and tasted excellent 👌

The owner made sure everyone is taken cared of. These kind of small cafés are hard to come by these days.

Highly recommend to check it out if you are visiting London area. 🙌👍

On to the next! 🫡

came here during the last day of our london adventure, met the lovely owner who made us DELICIOUS food, i even recieved a complimentary piece of white pudding because the owner wanted me to try it, so delicious. Paolo engaged in some lovely conversation about our travels and gave us tips for our upcoming days, such a lovely cosy place. would recommend some more coffee training but overall we would 100% return 🩷

The Almeida is one of the best producing theatres in London. Some of their shows go to the West End and Broadway.

They champion new work as well as bringing fresh perspectives to revivals.

The space is very flexible and can be a proscenium, of thrust, or even in the round, whatever is best for that particular play.

They don't shy away from controversial plays and musicals, so this is the place to go if you want to be challenged as well as entertained.

Tip: When you book seats downstairs it may say restricted view. That's because this is an old venue with poles holding up the balcony. As you can see in the picture there small and in my experience they don't really get in the way. If that bothers you the seats upstairs are excellent.

It was afternoon of performances from drama students doing extracts of theatre performed over the years at the almeida. Really high standard as I expected. And only £12 for 3.5 hours of theatre, pretty good. Some context about what was going on would be useful but still very entertaining.

First visit to the Almedia Theatre.
Front of House staff. Friendly and helpful.
Cafe/bar reasonable
I think the theatre is across  buildings. But the performance space in one.

Show: Portia Coughlan
A moving and difficult at times play. The cast is excellent. Especially, Alison Oliver as the titular Portia. The play set in Ireland through I've not met an Irish woman called Portia. Kathy Kiera Clarke is excellent in both comedic parts and the more serious elements of the play.
A very powerful play.

Second visit, 1536, set during the fall of Queen Anne Boleyn it charts a brief period of time in the lives of three Essex girls. Whilst socially by class divided from Anne. By sex their freedom to act as we understand that freedom.
The cast of two male actors supporting powerful performances from all three of the female leads.
The last act and conclusion comes as a shock. Hopefully a transfer to the a west End will follow. A must see.
Front of house and cafe/bar staff very good.

Third visit: A Moon for the Misbegotten, set in 1923 rural Connecticut tells the tale of an Irish immigrant farmer (David Threfellan) and his daughter Josie (Ruth Wilson) as they try to tame the land after his last son leaves  superficially about plots and intrigue, the play to me is about being authentic to self.
An excellent cast, studio dressing and direction.
I attended the relaxed performance which was interesting.

Theatre Tour, regular tours throughout the year. Excellent history, and view of behind scenes areas, and a step onto the stage (particular stage for Christmas Day). The guide Nick very knowledgeable and engaging. Definitely, worth doing.

Fourth show. Christmas Day. A Jewish father visits his children and partner at their unorthodox living space for Christmas Day. And weaves it way with Jewish humour and references as to how Jews mark Christmas. The play 1hr 50mins is a powerful piece.
The play as presented and written are different. The book contains an additional scene with a hitherto unseen flatmate returns (which was explained in the being as everyone else 12 we're at home). The deletion of the scene does not weaken the final play.

Sunday afternoon - boyyy the wait was long… but SO worth it!
Gentleman at the door handled everything with such a controlled and calm demeanour.
Staff inside were very nice and pleasant.
BUT THE FOOD! The food hit the spot! Perfectly seasoned and flavours were so good! Im not usually this impressed.
The food came out quick too. Would highly recommend getting the halloumi fries.
Coffees were also spot on!
My first proper brunch since moving to London and I’m so glad it was here. Well done to the owners and management.

Will definitely be returning InshaAllah

⸻

This is honestly one of the best breakfasts I’ve ever had. We’re regulars here, and because of that we’ve bumped into the owner a few times — him and my partner are now acquaintances. When we come in and he’s there, he always comes over to us personally, which just adds to how welcoming the place feels.

I usually go for the Wild West breakfast with the Milieu signature beans, and those beans are incredible. I have genuinely never had beans this good anywhere else — they’re rich, flavourful, and elevate the whole breakfast.

The hash browns are perfectly crispy on the outside and soft and fluffy on the inside. The pancakes are delicious — light, fluffy, and served with fresh strawberries and berries every time.

I’ve also tried the mac and cheese bites, and they’re a nice option if you love cheese. Definitely worth trying.

For drinks, I usually get an iced caramel latte, which I always enjoy — it’s consistently great.

The customer service is also amazing! The staff are so friendly and attentive.

Overall, I’d rate this place a 10 out of 10. There’s nothing to fault; everything is perfection. If you’re looking for a new breakfast spot, this is definitely the place to check out.

Incredible food, staff are welcoming & friendly. Customers need to be mindful of the wait time & are required to join a minimum 1hr queue over the weekends after 10am regardless of group size.

Overall, great time & atmosphere within.

All our dishes were the chef’s signature.

Thank you to Yunis & his team on Sunday, 1st Feb 2026.

ChIJteLYIO8cdkgRxYGV9AfNElI.name: London Fields Lido
ChIJteLYIO8cdkgRxYGV9AfNElI.address: London Fields West Side, London E8 3EU, UK

reviews.text: Amazing 50m outdoor pool open year round. Very well maintained and clean locker rooms. Lanes can get very busy and chaotic in the summer with novice swimmers, but it’s worth it for the outdoor space.

A great local, heated lido - well maintained, good and friendly staff and a mix of regulars, locals and passers through. It feels part of the local landscape and part of the local community.

Personally I love a winter's swim when the steam is rising and the air is crisp and the sky a pure blue - but it's great at anytime of the year.

It is unsurprisingly very busy on a hot summers day - but still swimmable if you come with patience and a forgiving attitude.

This place is lovely, first of all it being outdoors is a really nice change, you don’t get too hot and it’s great to be outside.
The pool is always immaculately clean, quite impressive there’s never any leaves etc in there, water is really clear. Always warm enough, great changing facilities and plenty of them. Staff are really amazing, so friendly and great instructors too. I just love this place, hope nothing ever changes!

ChIJ3QxXoloABDQRlw86bPG_6EI.name: Queen Elizabeth Stadium
ChIJ3QxXoloABDQRlw86bPG_6EI.address: 18號 Oi Kwan Rd, Wan Chai, Hong Kong

reviews.text: On 24 May 2025, my wife and I came here to watch Nasatoshi Nakamura’s concert in Hong Kong. Whilst he is just a few years older than me, the star and singer-songwriter personified a TV drama hero that I adored in the mid-1970s. It was a full house and the audience were mostly senior citizens. The Stadium’s location and facilities are user-friendly.

the setting of sound is good here and it’s a good place for holding a small concert here, first time watching a Cappella and it’s fun haha and people’s voice is so powerful the music can be so complicate even without music instruments haaa

rating of mine: 4

Beautiful Stadium/arena with plenty of space for spectators and center stage floor for competitions of any kind. The lighting equipment is very nice and professional resulting in great photographies taken. Changing rooms are also quite spacious and comfortable with mirrors and lights for better makeup sessions.

Such a lovely spot at Shoreditch! ☕✨ I really enjoyed my time here. The coffee was delicious and perfectly made, and the breakfast was fresh, tasty, and very satisfying. You can tell they really care about quality.
I also loved the concept and overall vibe of the place — it feels cozy and thoughtfully designed. Even the menu design is super stylish and adds to the whole experience.
Definitely a place I’d come back to and highly recommend!

I came to this place with a friend at around lunchtime on a Saturday. The place is not big, rather cosy, and most tables were occupied during the time we were there, but not crazy crowded. My friend had a savoury breakfast and I had sweet pancakes with fruit and ice-cream. It's not super fancy food, but everything looked great with decent portions. I really enjoyed my pancakes and would definitely go back to have them again. They were soft and almost crispy outside, and the combination with the fruit, ice cream and syrup made it very tasty. We also had a coffee and it was a great coffee for my taste, on the sweet side rather than bitter or acidic. We paid around 20 pound each, which is pretty good for London and we liked everything. I would recommend and go back to this place.

My favourite breakfast spot in Shoreditch. The main dishes also looked great, but since I went in the morning, I had the chance to try their breakfast — absolutely worth it.

If you’re ever around Old Street, I’d definitely recommend stopping by. I enjoyed a lovely breakfast while soaking in the warm atmosphere inside and the view outside. I had the halloumi, avocado and roasted vegetables on sourdough, and it was absolutely delicious, highly recommend.

I also really liked the overall vibe of the café and the background music — it all added to the experience. The staff were attentive and the service was excellent, thank you! I’ll definitely be back.

We had breakfast here on the first day of our London trip and absolutely loved it! Naturally, we had to come back a few days later. I highly recommend the Eggs Benedict, Çılbır, and the Turkish breakfast—they were all delicious. The staff were incredibly kind. Thank you for everything! 🌸💕

The food was so good! The staff are so lovely ! There were some halal options, you just need to ask the staff. We had the Mediterranean breakfast and the morning turkey breakfast. Love that you have options of sweet and savoury breakfast! And it comes with a good variety. We just walked around and found this cute little place close to Islington for lunch. The food is also decently price - cheap for a central London brunch spot and it actually tastes good and fills you up. Found our new fav little hideaway cafe ! They have the cutest decor around!

Absolutely amazing! The breakfast was outstanding – every little detail was carefully thought out. The quality, flavor, and presentation were simply perfect, and the prices are unbeatable.
I’ve traveled a lot around the world, but I’ve never had such a delicious and lovingly prepared breakfast for this kind of price.
A special shout-out to the waitress, who was incredibly kind, attentive, and genuinely sweet – especially with children. She treats them with such warmth and even gives them a lovely little surprise that makes the experience even more special.
I can highly recommend this place – it’s the perfect way to start your day!

We had our Wedding breakfast and celebrations in Fidelio and it was the best decision we made for our wedding without a doubt. I’m so so happy I found Fidelio!

Ash is an incredible Manager and made every effort to help make our wedding vision come to life.

On the day everything went perfectly and the food was absolutely delicious. Our family and friends loved Fidelio and we’ve had so many people tell us that they had a great evening there.

Photos by Nicola Drummond Photography
The best bit about choosing Fidelio for your wedding/party is that you’ll be able to go back and relive it by just visiting the cafe!

Thank you Ash & team for giving us the best wedding day we could have ever imagined!!!

I recently hosted my leaving doo at Fidelio and it couldn’t have gone better.

The manger, Ash, was fantastic from start to finish. He completely understood the vibe I was hoping for and went above and beyond to make it special for my guests and me. Nothing was too much trouble. Everything felt easy, relaxed and genuinely well looked after.

The food was great, the space is beautiful, cosy and unique. The whole evening just flowed. It felt intimate without being formal, and special without being overdone.

I would 100% recommend this venue for a event or party.

Lovely cafe that I stumbled across on a recent work trip. The scrambled eggs were fantastic with a great atmosphere. Relaxing jazzy playlist and suitable for working there with no rush to leave. Good coffee too!
One of my favorite high schools I attended
WONDERFUL SCHOOL = THE BEST EDUCATION IN LONDON!
Wrong school in the wrong place. No respect for the fact it was a school in Britain, or the culture around it.

Hyde Park is one of the largest and most famous parks in London. It is located in the center of the city and is a popular place for relaxation and outdoor activities. People visit Hyde Park to walk, ride bicycles, have picnics, or enjoy nature. The park is also known for Speakers’ Corner, where people can freely express their opinions. Hyde Park is an important green space and a favorite destination for both locals and tourists.

Hyde Park is a great place to relax in the middle of London. It’s spacious, well maintained, and perfect for walking, sitting by the lake, or just taking a break from the busy city. I enjoyed the calm atmosphere, open green spaces, and the mix of locals and tourists. A must-visit if you want some fresh air and peace in London.

Hyde Park is such a relaxing and beautiful place in the middle of London. It’s perfect for walking, chilling, or just taking a break from the busy city.
The park is huge, green, and peaceful, and it’s great in any season. Whether you want to walk around, sit by the water, or enjoy some fresh air, it’s always a good idea

I visited this location a few times while in Richmond. It's very cozy and the staff are very nice. You can dine inside or outside at the many tables. The toilets are clean, just ask the staff for the code for the door. One of the best cups of coffee I had while in the UK.

Great cafe tucked under the Richmond Bridge with a beautiful scenic view of the river and the old three. Had a nice flat white and a spinach feta pastry which was delicious. Excellent vegetarian options both sweet and savoury. Plenty of outdoor seating tables for reading with the coffee or just simply people watching by the river.
Nice cafe by the river under the bridge. It's small inside but it has plenty tables outside if warm enough to sit outside.

ChIJL7zxE_IMdkgRCtW5GSlFJCU.name: World Rugby Museum & Allianz Stadium Tours
ChIJL7zxE_IMdkgRCtW5GSlFJCU.address: Allianz Stadium, 200 Whitton Rd, Twickenham TW2 7BA, UK

reviews.text: What a great stadium tour, so much history to see as well as a true modern rugby stadium. Iconic does not come close.

Great experience at the home of England Rugby. The tour of the stadium was comprehensive and the guide was very knowledgeable. For any sports fan this is a must do. Short walk from Twickenham Station.
Brilliant. Very interesting. The tour was fantastic and our guide, Mike, very pleasant and knowledgeable

ChIJJzYMYAAPdkgRedNXVqRKW1A.name: Moo cafe Restaurant persian
ChIJJzYMYAAPdkgRedNXVqRKW1A.address: 42 Fulham Palace Rd, London W6 9PH, UK

reviews.text:
This is a local hidden gem. Had such a wonderful experience. The staff were extremely friendly and the owner made the experience seem so personal.
The food was delicious and great  coffee! The atmosphere is peaceful and serene, providing a lovely escape from the hustle and bustle. Highly recomended.

I absolutely loved this cafe! The coffee was rich and flavorful, and the cakes were freshly made and so delicious.
The staff were incredibly kind and welcoming  they made me feel at home.
The atmosphere was cozy and relaxing with such a positive vibe
Such a wonderful little gem - the owners are so friendly, cinnamon buns are delicious and the best part is the hospitality. Feels like visiting a friend’s home - thank you for the smiles! ♥️

ChIJtSERLYkOdkgRSGdUwBMSD1k.name: Euro Cafe (Full English Breakfast, Pancakes, Coffee & Juices)
ChIJtSERLYkOdkgRSGdUwBMSD1k.address: Dry Cleaners, 45 Sheen Ln, London SW14 8AB, UK

reviews.text:
Popped in here for a coffee early last week.

Had a really warm welcome in this early opening cafe.

Situated very near the train station, it had a little charm in the decor with a selection of vintage wireless sets in the wall.

Coffee was good.

Had a television with the news on so one can start the day well informed.

Food did look ok as I glanced at the ither diners and there were many others there.

This would be a good place to check out if you are in the area and need a bite if pick me up.

The breakfast is so good here. The coffee is great and the bread they used for my toast was really light and toasted perfectly. Highly recommend this place. The staff are also lovely. Great place very chilled and really good value for money

I am so pleased to find just a normal cafe! Prices are not inflated to some 'boutique' cafes, and the food is top-notch. I haven't been able to find a good old bubble and squeak on a menu for a long time! Oh, and it was spot-on, as was the bacon, eggs cooked to my taste, tomatoes, and sausage. A lovely couple running the place, too. My breakfast set me up for the rest of my day! Even managed to park, literally, right outside for free - another rarity. Thank you.

Incredible! We had heard good things about this place. There was a bit of a queue but we decider to wait it out. Certainly glad we did! The queue went down really quick and the food was incredible!! All 4 of us have said how nice the food was and we all had different dishes. A lot of choice and wish we could visit more often to try it all. 5/5!!

This was probably the most enjoyable place I visited in England, both in terms of the time I spent and the food I had. When I learned that the owner is Turkish, I wasn’t surprised at all that the place is this good and well run. The hospitality and genuine care we received were truly delightful.

The salmon was the best I’ve ever had, incredibly tender and buttery. We were told it’s smoked using traditional methods, and the quality really shows. It’s a busy spot, and for good reason. Once you go, you immediately want to come back.

I will definitely visit again next time I’m in the area. If I’m remembering the names correctly, special thanks to Çınar and Şahin. It was a pleasure meeting you.

Had breakfast at Drury Covent Garden this morning and honestly it was a lot better than I expected for such a quick stop. I wasn’t looking for anything big, just something light to start the day, so I went with a croissant, another coffee, and a grilled egg. Sounds simple, but the whole thing actually came together really nicely.

The place itself has a relaxed vibe, kind of that “London café but not trying too hard” feel. People were just doing their own thing — some working, some chatting, some half-asleep like me — and it didn’t feel chaotic or rushed at all. The staff were chilled too, not overly chatty but friendly in that normal, human way. They took my order, kept it moving, and didn’t make it awkward.

The croissant was warm and soft on the inside with just enough flakiness on the outside, but not the kind that makes crumbs fly everywhere. The grilled egg was actually really good, cooked properly and tasted fresh, not rubbery or dry like you sometimes get. And the coffee… honestly, that was the best part. Strong enough to wake me up but still smooth. Not bitter, not burnt, just solid.

I sat there for a little while and the whole breakfast just felt easy and enjoyable. Nothing fancy, nothing dramatic, just a decent morning meal that hit exactly right. I’d happily swing by Drury Covent Garden again for another quick breakfast like that.

ChIJO1vhVbAEdkgRPoJKQ9gTOh4.name: The London Nautical School
ChIJO1vhVbAEdkgRPoJKQ9gTOh4.address: London Nautical School, 61 Stamford St, London SE1 9NA, UK

ChIJD7FsstMadkgRbjUI2TpkHAs.name: The London Welbeck Hospital
ChIJD7FsstMadkgRbjUI2TpkHAs.address: The London Welbeck Hospital, 27 Welbeck St, London W1G 8EN, UK

reviews.text:
If i could rate The Wellbeck with 10 stars, I would. I feel all staff have gone above and beyond and made me feel instantly at ease, safe and cared for.
The reason I am compelled to write this review is a very special nurse called Esther. Her clinical skills have been outstanding, from explaining safety / reasons why I had been prescribed pain medication to why I needed to complete certain health checks that otherwise I might have not prioritised. Her levels of care are the best I’ve ever received. Esther is a truly outstanding nurse, despite me stating I was “ fine” when under the influence of medication, she knew the signs that indicated in wasn’t and acted calmly but safely and got me to my bed before I tainted. I could continue, I don’t have enough words to give this incredible lady justice.

There are some other people that also massively impacted my patient experience, Julie the anaesthetist, her skills and warmth absolutely helped me to feel informed, safe and cared for, from discussing lifestyle factors that could influence my health outcomes to explaining what would happen on the way in to the operating room. Pain management- excellent too.

Ida, who took care of me through my admission, helping me settle in, she was calm, reassuring, she explained after care beautifully and most importantly made me feel like she was caring for me like a family member would. She demonstrated integrity, she didn’t answer my questions with vagueness or any generic detail, she answered with honesty and transparency.

There was a HCPN, I forgot her name, i briefly met her but she was also wonderful, caring and had a fantastic bedside manner.
She reassured me I was in safe hands.

Finally Mr Marsh, simply incredible, his clinical skills speak for themselves, however, he really took me by surprise with his warmth, caring and attention to detail. He listened to me, his care was extremely attentive and individual to me. He’s hands down the most amazing consultant I have met.

A truly outstanding and wonderful experience throughout a time of vulnerability and life changing surgery.

Thank you to each and every one you, I am very grateful for this amazing experience.

I had the most amazing experience at the Wellbeck Hospital as a day patient.Every member of staff i had dealings with, so professional and caring. Special thank you to my nurse Ida what a lovely lady and so interesting to talk too.
Also the lad on reception who helped me into my Uber.

I would highly recommend this hospital its a pearl in the middle of the west end.

Keep up the good work.

Ann from epsom

My wife attended the Welbeck hospital on 12.12.25 for breast augmentation. Receptionist wasn't very friendly when greeting just passed clipboard like fill this out, could of be more welcoming considering its private care. The sugeon Dr Lahoud was brilliant as always and the surgery room team. Facilities clean and comfortable. Wanted to sat a big thank you to Nurse Takeeasy, she was friendly caring attentive professional and kind an asset to the team and really reassured my wife throughout.

ChIJK33klGIbdkgRRsVI74lkGmk.name: joon cafe
ChIJK33klGIbdkgRRsVI74lkGmk.address: joon, 56 Warren St, London W1T 5NP, UK

reviews.text: Best brunch place in London! Best soya cappuccino! The flavors are really unique and everything we tried was super tasty. You can tell they use fresh ingredients, which makes a big difference in the quality of the food and drinks. The atmosphere is warm and friendly, making it a perfect place to have brunch and enjoy a good coffee. Definitely a spot I’d come back to again!

this place is my ultimate favourite. i bookmarked it even before my trip to london cause of great reviews. when i finally got the chance to try their pistachio pancakes and THE MOST AMAZING CAPPUCCINO it sent me into orbit. i’ll definitely be coming back every time i am in london.

Phenomenal breakfast place! Perhaps the best we've tried in London after a few years!  We had the veggie poached eggs on sourdough (Better Florentine) and pistachio pancakes plus 2 different matcha. The roasted veggies were heavenly. Matcha was great not overly sweet. Lynn was wonderful as our server introducing dishes and drinks. It does get very busy, and maybe another server is needed to manage all the tables.
Just a popup for G dragon concert.
1 of the best.

So so so so good, lucky to sit on CAT3 and see the super beautiful whole scene, but the camera is bad and can't zoom in very clearly, quick entrance, great faceid, great sound and lighting throughout the program!!

ChIJZf1BrahC04kRImpwIiI96xg.name: Sal Maglie Stadium
ChIJZf1BrahC04kRImpwIiI96xg.address: 1201 Hyde Park Blvd, Niagara Falls, NY 14301, USA

reviews.text:
Great place to watch baseball!!! 85degrees today so bring your sunscreen and get there early. Bucket seats are limited and provide a bit of shade behind home plate.
It can be windy at times too.

Son Played in  a 13 u baseball game here. Kids loved it and felt like the big leagues to them!

If you are playing at 12 noon on a hot day bring an umbrella as there is limited shade.

Pro tip…If you are with a tournament we set up our tents on the first base side just past the stands because the bleachers were a bit toasty to last  the whole game.

There are restrooms.

Cannot rate food or if there is any, ours was a youth tourney.

The Stadium is beautiful! It's clean and has plenty of seating. Parking is available in a lot next to Stadium but street parking is also available. They have a concession stand. Beer garden is available too.

Theatre is pretty compact but does the job. Lion king show was GREAT!!! Fairly priced food and drinks. Souvenirs prices are crazy though. I recommend keeping jacket with you instead of using cloakroom - it is pretty chilly inside (during winter). Staff is very helpful. We loved the visit!

Watching The Lion King at the Lyceum Theatre is an experience that begins the moment you walk through the doors. The theatre itself is absolutely beautiful, with a classic and elegant architecture that immediately sets the tone for the night. The seats are very comfortable, the layout is well designed, and the overall atmosphere is fantastic, full of excitement and anticipation.

The show is truly extraordinary. From the very first scene, you are completely drawn in by the power of the music, the creativity of the staging, and the incredible energy of the production. Every single actor delivers an outstanding performance, combining strong vocals, expressive movement, and genuine emotion. The music is powerful and immersive, creating moments that give you goosebumps and stay with you long after the curtain falls.

The production quality is exceptional. The costumes, choreography, and visual effects are all executed with incredible attention to detail, making the story come alive in a magical and unforgettable way. Everything flows seamlessly, keeping you engaged from beginning to end and making time fly by.

This is definitely something you should not miss if you are visiting London. More than just a musical, it is a complete and memorable experience that captures the heart and reminds you why live theatre is so special. An absolute must see.

Lyceum Theatre – London

A very beautiful and classic theatre with so much character. The interior is stunning and really gives that old-school London theatre charm.

The only downside for me was that it felt a bit warm inside — would be even better with stronger air-conditioning, especially when the theatre is full.

That aside, the experience was amazing. This was my first time watching The Lion King, and it absolutely blew me away. The costumes, stage design, live music, and performers were incredible — you can really see the full effort and passion put into the show.

A truly memorable experience and definitely worth watching at least once in your life.

⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️ (minus one tiny star for the heat 😄)

A really beautiful minor league ballpark. Kind of a throwback: no cursing, no booing, no disorderly conduct. Just a great crowd in a great ballpark having a terrific time. Probably seats around 6000 people. Clear sight lines no matter where you sit.  Plenty of parking. We had a really good time. ♿️

We always have a great time at the Rumble Ponies games—it’s truly the highlight of our summer! The stadium is clean and well maintained, with several food and drink options. I especially love the liquor/wine slushy stand—so good! They do a great job with themed nights and giveaways, and Dog Day is always a favorite! Parking is easy, cheap, and super convenient. The staff has always been pleasant and helpful too. My only wish is to see the mascots out more often, but I get that those costumes get hot! We’re already looking forward to our next game!!

Mirabito Stadium is a nice place to see a baseball game! Sightlines are excellent from nearly every seat in the park. You are never too far away from the action. During warmups, it is easy to see and even talk to the players. The stadium does offer nice views of the surrounding hills and you can also see some buildings in downtown Binghamton. The historic Marconi Tower is right next to the stadium and is also visible from the stadium.
The stadium offers excellent food choices. Lupo’s Grill offers spiedies; a regional classic. Off the right field line, there is a nice deck you can watch the game from while having food or drink. New this year is a large video scoreboard in right field. There are also some picnic tables behind the right field wall. Food prices are typically high, but the prices are comparable to other minor league ballparks. The Rumble Ponies have a team store near the front of the stadium that offers an extensive selection of Rumble Ponies merchandise.
Ticket prices are very reasonable and you can get good seats for a low price. The team provided plenty of entertainment and prize activities during breaks in the action. It is best to park in a paid parking lot during the game. Parking spots are available near the stadium, but there are a lot of foul balls that leave the stadium and hit cars that are parked near the stadium.
All and all you can’t go wrong at Mirabito Stadium. It offers good seating at fair prices with a chance to see the MLB players of tomorrow.

Stopped for a quick breakfast during my last trip to NYC. The staff were so nice and helpful. Ordered these very indulgent pancakes that were tasty. They had lots of fresh ingredients. The prices had increased compared to other reviews photos I had seen. The prices were still very reasonable. Would definitely visit again.

I was looking for breakfast place not far from our hotel in Manhattan.  I saw in Maps there was a deli at the corner of the block we were staying in.  So the next morning we walked down to the deli at the corner of West 54th and 8th Avenue.  Walked right up to the counter and saw examples of what we wanted (like the plastic models of food we see in Japan).  We ordered two small Mochas which were freshly steamed and a few minutes later we were enjoying our breakfast.

It’s a small space but ample for a quick meal - and the food was very good.  I had French Toast with bacon - the toast was good and thick, battered just right and sprinkled with powdered sugar - the bacon was not skimpy but appropriately thick and more of a center cut with a good amount of meat (rather than fat and gristle).  My wife had two eggs over easy served on avocado toast with lettuce and tomato.  A hearty meal to start a day of sightseeing.

We came back another day later in the week and shared a croissant breakfast sandwich - the croissant was very fresh and the combo was tasty.

Since I arrived in NYC, I was searching for a main place to get a good experience of a American breakfast. This place just stole my food heart! Yesterday I took pancakes with bananas and strawberries. It was so good that I had to eat here again this morning. I recommend this place to everyone who wants to not overpay for food and wants to enjoy an true American breakfast! (and yes, the food you’re seeing on the second picture was all for me 😝)

Had breakfast with my work friends and we celebrated one of our friends birthday. Food is divine. Their burritos and eggs benedict,  you would want more. Service was fantastic too and a very cozy ambiance. Perfect for family and friends. We asked for a dessert that we can use for blowing of candles but they dont have,  instead they offered lemon ricotta pancakes,  it was good too and they have it on the house. so nice of them. Highly recommend and will definitely come back.

I went to this cafe twice back to back days and it had lovely vibes. It was decked out with decor for the holidays which shows the care and creativity the staff have for the ambiance. Second, the food was 10/10 both times, I got the New York bagel, and the breakfast burrito and both were delicious and huge portions. Lastly, the service was great. I had Jennifer as a server and she was very kind and attentive to me which I always appreciate when sitting down to eat. Check it out next time you’re in the area!

Visiting NY and this was my last meal in the Big Apple... such a wonderful experience! I loved every bite! 🏆🤩 The atmosphere is a pure delight. The Holiday spirit totally surrounded us, musically, visually, even the menu! I enjoyed a special Mistletoe cocktail to the last drop ☺️ Beautiful spot to soak in a new, delicious New York. 👏🏼☺️👏🏼☺️

Penn Station is the hub that keeps New York moving. It’s always full of energy, busy, crowded and maybe not the prettiest station in the world but it’s highly functional for travelers who can use Subway, AmTrak and LIRR services from here to get where they need to go. The food court has a variety of options, from Shake Shack to Raising Cane’s, from Chopt to Playa Bowls and there’s a Walgreens that can pretty much help every needs one may have while waiting to commute or travel around New York! I’m personally here every day and I must say that Pennsylvania Station is safe, with a lot of MTA Police constantly around and clean for the high amount of traffic that passes through daily. It’s I place in town that I love! Don’t expect luxury, especially during rush hours, but Penn Station does the job!

We're always just a quick in and out of this place so I can't really review the facilities in its entirety. There are several fast food eateries and shops. We did stop at one of the food shops to grab some drinks & chips before getting onboard. But what little we experienced, it was fine. The only gripe would be better signage for track numbers.
Beautiful architecture. A must see place while visiting Manhattan, NY city. Fun place with great historical significance especially for railroad inthusiasts.

ChIJhcA7rstZwokR2UOjvUcQPkQ.name: L B Coffee
ChIJhcA7rstZwokR2UOjvUcQPkQ.address: 454 E 84th St Store Front, New York, NY 10028, USA

reviews.text: Fantastic coffee! Fabulous vibes! I’m just glad this nice coffee shop is in the neighborhood. They treat pets & their own staff as kind as clients. You can tell the owner put a lot thoughts.

STUNNING coffee shop located on the UES. It’s spotless and has such a cozy, tranquil energy. This is the kind of coffee shop you would have a meet cute in and bump into the love of your life. The coffee and food is top notch. You can tell the owners put a lot of thought into the quality of products sold here. If you’re into flavored coffee, definitely try the syrups. They have such a wide selection and they’re small batch & organic. Service is also excellent. The guys in the front are some of the warmest people I’ve ever encountered in NYC.

Such a cute cafe!! The Zatar croissants were heaven - they’re from Italy! I loved the latte and affogato with gelato. Omg the Zatar bread was amazing. I’m so excited to try their labneh and hummus next time. HIGHLY recommend!!

Out of all the museums I’ve visited so far, this one feels the most comfortable to me. Maybe I’m just a fan of naïve and so-called “degenerate” art — but there’s something about this place that feels intimate rather than overwhelming.

You’re not flooded with an impossible volume of works to rush through. The curation is thoughtful — the placement, the sequence, the dialogue between paintings and decorative objects all feel intentional and balanced. You can actually breathe and take it in.

I was especially in awe of the architectural design itself. The building — a former Gilded Age mansion on Museum Mile — adds another layer to the experience. It feels refined yet warm.

The collection of German and Austrian art and design is exceptional, featuring works by artists like Gustav Klimt (including Portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer I), Egon Schiele, and Wassily Kandinsky, alongside pieces from the Wiener Werkstätte and Bauhaus movements. It’s a powerful combination of fine art and design.

I also loved that you can dine inside. If the café on the first floor is full, there’s another option downstairs — equally charming.

Just note: no photography in the galleries. And honestly, that’s part of the beauty. You’re forced to slow down, look closely, and let the works speak to you.

Loved every part of it.

Beautiful museum of German and Austrian art on Museum Mile of 5th Ave. The building itself is historic and a work of art, and the collection is stunning. Lovely gift shops, a cafe, and friendly staff make this a very worth while stop on your art sight seeing tour. I went for a guided tour and learned a lot, saw provocative and thought provoking pieces and was impressed at the variety in each exhibit. 10/10 highly recommend.

Located on the upper east side right across from Central Park, I had no expectations other than reviewers complaining about staff harassing them.  You enter via an unimpressive side entrance and immediately must go through a security check - the staff were friendly but stern - no liquids allowed to enter at all (we were forced to drink our bottled water on the sidewalk).  Once inside you purchase tickets from a very friendly staff person ($28, similar to the $30 at MET, MOMA, and Frick) and are directed to complimentary coat check in the basement.  You are may WEAR your coat / jacket/ sweatshirt but the moment you take any of these off, their security / staff will immediately tell you to put them back on or take them to coat check.  FYI - we found the building to be very warm in the winter.    No photographs allowed anywhere and the minute you take out your phone, they immediately have eyes on what you are doing.   That said, they do have some nice audio commentary for many of their pieces so it is worthwhile to download the app (FREE) and bring your earbuds.  Their rules are published so I suppose fair game for them to so vigilantly enforce them, but it is the most strict of any museum I have been to and did make the whole visit a little less relaxing (if not for myself, than for other people always being reprimanded). Do yourself a favor and just plan to follow the rules.  While the museum in an older home, it definitely has more a museum vibe than a luxurious mansion vibe. Overall we had a nice 30-40 minute visit - this isn't a large collection.  Yes they have some amazing Klimts, as well as some other nice pieces......but if you aren't a big Klimt fan, I am not sure I would go out of my way or pay  this relatively higher price (considering the smaller collection) compared to other much more varied and larger volume at the other museums.   I am glad I went and had a nice visit (big Klimt fan) but can't see myself making much effort to return very often.

ChIJp8ENSlRYwokRIIpmnGxjieo.name: Lunt-Fontanne Theatre
ChIJp8ENSlRYwokRIIpmnGxjieo.address: 205 W 46th St, New York, NY 10036, USA

reviews.text:
This was my very first Broadway show, so this review is from a total novice but I absolutely loved it.

We arrived about 10 minutes late due to a train delay, but the show hadn’t started and the staff was incredibly kind and efficient in getting us to our seats. The theater was packed, and we were the last two to be seated. We sat in the middle mezzanine, and thanks to the sloped layout, our view was completely unobstructed. Honestly, there isn’t a bad seat in the house.

The sound and lighting were excellent, and the scene transitions were seamless, everything felt effortless and magical. Restrooms are available on both the upstairs and lower levels; skip the upstairs lines and head downstairs.

Overall, a fantastic experience and perfect for solo, date, family, or group outings. I can’t wait to return!

Death Becomes Her is pure Broadway joy, funny, flashy, and wildly entertaining from the first moment to the final bow.

The show is laugh-out-loud hilarious, packed with sharp comedy and deliciously over the top moments that know exactly what they’re doing. The leading ladies are absolutely incredible. Jennifer Simard and Betsy Wolfe are a dream pairing, fearless, funny, vocally thrilling, and completely committed. Their chemistry is electric, and they bring the perfect mix of camp, heart, and star power. And Michelle Williams is spectacular. She commands the stage with confidence, warmth, and powerhouse presence, adding another layer of excitement to an already phenomenal cast.

The costumes are stunning, bold, glamorous, and jaw-dropping. Every look feels like a showstopper. The lighting is absolutely on point, elevating the musical and enhancing the magic with precision and style.

Death Becomes Her is Broadway at its most fun, big laughs, incredible performances, and a visual feast. If you want a night of pure entertainment with performers at the top of their game, this show is a must-see.

This theatre is stunning 😍 definitely recommend seeing a show here. 🎭
Not a bad seat in the house 🙌
The bar offers show themed drinks and sell show merch right next to that!
Chandeliers, gold accents, and crown molding take you to another time. 👑

ChIJA1RM8MAB1IkRuGqmplyHSo8.name: Eunice Kennedy Shriver Stadium
ChIJA1RM8MAB1IkRuGqmplyHSo8.address: 350 New Campus Dr, Brockport, NY 14420, USA

reviews.text: Member of Class of 2007, have enjoyed coming out now and then. They do offer a great opportunity to watch multiple sports here. Concession stand, they offer different foods and drinks.

Awful. One set of bleechers completely shoveled and dry. Our side full of snow where people could slip and fall. Stairs full of snow. Bleechers full of snow.
Bathrooms were warm. People friendly tho.
Exciting and loving everyones enthusiasm!

Reviewing this cafe for its orignal flaky soft, puffy thin layered doughy old fashion NYC croissants. Came here after a 6 am arrival to NYC, enjoyed the morning atmosphere and spotted the crossiants fresh out of the oven. Guy baking and at register was so kind and didn't hesistate when i asked to get a fresh one, he even offered to warm it up but i declined, orignal NYC croissants dont need rewarming.

Service at the counter is polite, efficient and warm/welcoming.

Upstairs seating is plentiful and quiet, but could use more attention from staff as far as cleanliness goes.

Food is good.

Picture is view from upstairs seating.

Absolutely loved this place! The combination of freshly baked pastries and authentic pizza is a dream come true. The pizza had a perfect crispy crust with rich, flavorful toppings—clearly made with quality ingredients. As for the pastries, they were just heavenly—light, flaky, and not overly sweet. You can tell everything is made with care. The atmosphere is cozy, and the staff were warm and welcoming. Whether you’re craving something savory or sweet, this spot has you covered. Highly recommend!

ChIJoeiVuf9YwokRYsfm2kqoTi0.name: Cafe Manhattan
ChIJoeiVuf9YwokRYsfm2kqoTi0.address: 35 W 45th St # A, New York, NY 10036, USA

reviews.text:
I stayed at a hotel in Times Square, and this place was just a 5-6 minute walk away—what a gem! The variety of food here is absolutely incredible, offering something for everyone. It’s one of those classic New York weigh-and-pay buffets, but the selection is beyond anything I’ve seen.

They’ve got sushi, burgers, breakfast classics, and even breakfast options from other cuisines. There’s a huge range of hot dishes, fresh fruits, and so much more. Honestly, I wish this place was right below my apartment—I could eat here every day and never get bored! The variety is so extensive that it surpasses any other restaurant I’ve been to in the U.S.

The staff are super hardworking and efficient, keeping everything moving quickly. The place is spotlessly clean, with plenty of seating, including a separate dining area upstairs. You can easily have all your meals here—breakfast, lunch, dinner, plus grab-and-go snacks for convenience. It’s perfect for anyone staying in Times Square, and it’s so much cheaper than sit-down restaurants or ordering à la carte. I can’t recommend this place enough—it’s a total game-changer for dining in the area!

I want to address an issue regarding this business. The food is good, but unfortunately the customer service is very disappointing. The girl working at the counter is rude and unprofessional, and her behavior is unacceptable. She often gives off a judgmental attitude, as if customers are a burden to her. It honestly seems like she does not enjoy working there.

The first time this happened, I came in during my break since I work in the building next door. I come here quite often, almost every day. That day I wasn’t feeling well and just wanted to sit on the second floor for a few minutes. After about 10 minutes, she approached me and told me I had to buy something or leave, and even said she would call the police if I didn’t. I understand that businesses may have policies, and I respect that. However, there is a professional and respectful way to communicate those policies, especially in the hospitality industry.

The second incident happened not long ago when I politely asked if there was a charger available. She completely ignored me and treated me as if I were asking for something unreasonable. The way she handled the situation made me feel uncomfortable and disrespected.

I believe management should pay closer attention to customer service standards. Good food alone is not enough to maintain a successful business if customers feel unwelcome.
Got a lunch roll set and added the miso soup for $2.95 with the promo. The sushi was fresh, the soup was warm and comforting. Great lunch combo for the price.

ChIJw4NeUACxNTERZ60FRIi0pVU.name: Cafe Chăm
ChIJw4NeUACxNTERZ60FRIi0pVU.address: RWFP+PF, Khoai Chau, Hung Yen, Vietnam

reviews.text:
A great place for a weekend with friends and family.
Attentive and enthusiastic staff.

The drinks here are really delicious. My favorite is the apricot cold brew – the coffee is light, fragrant, and the slight sourness of the apricot is very appealing. It's refreshing and not harsh. The apricot and rosehip tea is also extremely impressive; the natural fruit flavor, perfectly balanced sweet and sour, is very satisfying and easily addictive.

The cafe's ambiance is amazing; the atmosphere is relaxed and comfortable. The view is fantastic, perfect for chilling or working.

The staff are also great, enthusiastic, and pleasant.

Overall, this cafe is worth returning to many times 💛

The space here is airy, spacious, and clean. The drinks are just as good as those at the quality cafes I usually frequent in Hanoi. There's even a play area for children; at home, my kids love jumping on the sofa, so they can use up their energy in this play area. The food here suits my family's taste, and it comes out quickly.

Lagom - Book & Coffee
A peaceful haven for retirees,
A connecting space for young people,
And a whole new world for little book lovers!

Discovered a beautiful new book cafe
Located right near the lake, next to Ocean Park
The cafe has a wide variety of books and stories in many different genres
The owner is enthusiastic, and the staff are lovely
This cafe isn't very well-known, but it's one of my favorites. It has a quiet atmosphere perfect for working, and all the drinks are delicious.

ChIJz2dn4pyjNTERjCpGp142lxA.name: Anh Coffee
ChIJz2dn4pyjNTERjCpGp142lxA.address: Đường Hoa Phượng Đỏ, Khu đô thị, Lạc Hồng Phúc, Phường, Mỹ Hào, Hưng Yên, Vietnam

reviews.text: A great place to go and have coffee. You can choose from any of their 4 floors, each coming with its own great views. The staff are friendly and helpful.

Anh Coffee in Lac Hong Phuc Urban Area is an ideal destination with its spacious, airy, and tastefully decorated modern space. The drink menu is incredibly diverse, ranging from traditional coffees to milk teas, smoothies, fresh juices, and even pastries, catering to all customer preferences. In particular, the drinks are meticulously prepared, delicious, and beautifully presented. This is a great place to work, study, or meet friends. Professional and friendly service is also a big plus. To enhance interaction, Anh Coffee could implement Tuneast, allowing customers to freely choose their favorite songs, creating a shared musical atmosphere and providing a more enjoyable experience.
💜A fantastic experience with a stunning view and super high-quality drinks!🤩🤩🤩

ChIJFTZsQEqsNTERUTg5xqg2nlQ.address: Ngõ, 1333, đường Đ. Giải Phóng, Pháp Vân, Hoàng Mai, Hà Nội, Vietnam

reviews.text: Good

History of Phap Van Parish
A Brief History

The Phap Van parish has existed for a long time. In the 1927 census of churches in the Western Tonkin Diocese, the Phap Van church was described as being 22 meters long and 9.5 meters wide.

In 2014, due to the lack of a church, Cardinal Peter Nguyen Van Nhon allowed the parish to celebrate Mass at the private residences of Mr. Dung and Mr. Huy. With a large population, spiritual needs were an indispensable part of their lives. In 2015, under the care of Father Joseph Nguyen Ngoc Hinh of Thinh Liet (Ke Set) parish, the Hanoi City People's Committee returned a corner of a pond to the parish, where the current Phap Van church is located. The parish made efforts to build a temporary shelter for celebrating Mass. From then on, all religious activities were celebrated. Since 2015, all Masses, including Holy Week Masses, have been celebrated at the parish. Despite the difficulties and cramped facilities, the parishioners' enthusiasm has been met and continues to grow.

As an area with a large migrant population, the number of people attending the liturgy has steadily increased. Especially since Archbishop Joseph Vu Van Thien visited and celebrated Mass at the end of 2018, the religious life in the parish has flourished. In 2020, the parish was preparing the necessary documents to request the Archbishop's approval for its elevation to parish status. On November 21, 2020, Archbishop Joseph signed the decision to establish the Phap Van parish, appointing Father Joseph Nguyen Ngoc Hinh as the parish priest of Yen Duyen and Phap Van.

PHAP VAN PARISH
1. Name: Phap Van Parish (Cathedral Deanery, Archdiocese of Hanoi)

2. Year of Establishment: The Phap Van parish has existed for over 100 years.

– 2015: Began rebuilding the parish, constructing a temporary church for celebrating Mass.

– 2020: Construction of the church.

– December 5, 2020: Officially elevated to parish status.

3. Title:

4. Patron Saint: Saint Anthony (of Padua)

5. Area: Approximately 1000 m²

6. Parishioners: Approximately 100 people

7. Parish Unit:

8. Associations:

– Anthony Society

– Teresa Choir

– Holy Family Choir

– Our Lady of La Vang Society

– Catechists and Youth Leaders

9. Parish Priest: Joseph Nguyen Ngoc Hinh

Source: nhathophapvan.org

​Review for "Jose's Burrito Bar" (operating out of this kitchen):
​Absolute health hazard.

I ordered a Grilled Steak Burrito and french fries from this virtual brand thru Seamless. The french fries were completely raw—no seasoning, no color, and still chilled at the tips as if they were just briefly microwaved.

​Far worse, the steak in the burrito was spoiled. I could feel the aftertaste of the rot by the second bite and had to spit it out. I ended up throwing the entire meal away to avoid food poisoning. Judging by other reviews, this kitchen is clearly not following basic food safety or storage standards, Avoid any brand operating out of 589 1st Ave.

I bought the cheeseburger with fries and after I ate the Food, it gave me diarrhea all night. The cook was not pleasant or welcoming. I could've died from this. But Thank God I survived. Not worth buying food from these people.

I ordered a hot cappuccino and a smoothie.  Did not get that. Instead I got hot coffee water and a juice that was just celery blended with water. I take accountability for not tasting it first before but at the same time the female employee could have been honest and ask questions if she was unsure. Waste of money. 😒 LL

ChIJS7-89FNYwokRqHU42APYyVI.name: New Amsterdam Theatre
ChIJS7-89FNYwokRqHU42APYyVI.address: 214 W 42nd St, New York, NY 10036, USA

reviews.text: The theater is beautiful but its seating area is not tall people friendly. I'm 5'9, 160lbs and my knees were pressed against the seat in front of me. The seats are also not wide enough. I felt like a sardine. The show was nice.

I want to elaborate on my opinion about the theatre.
The place is very beautiful, a bit old, but very typical of Broadway. It's a beautiful establishment; I recommend it for lovers of architecture and history. ++
The theater staff are super friendly and really Help us. +++
We saw the show, Aladdin, and it was simply breathtaking! Incredible staging, fabulous sets and magnificent acting. The conductor and the musicians deserve praise because without them there is no magic. +++++
The price is a bit confusing; the price of a seat in the same row varies, and the same seat isn't always available from one online service to another, the seat is not the same price. --
The rules of theatre are simply idiotic.
It is forbidden to enter with a Coca-Cola in your bag. They told us it's forbidden. But inside, all drinks are for sale, including alcohol, but obviously at three times the price. A major drawback in my opinion. For the price of the ticket, you could at least let me have the Coca-Cola I just bought outside. ------- For the direction and the rules !!!

It was a wonderful experience! I highly recommend watching Aladdin—you’ll truly enjoy yourself. I had a great time, and sitting in the balcony section was a great choice because I could see the stage perfectly. My next Broadway show to watch is The Lion King.

ChIJ81TWXQZdwokR1J1tvpkGRuo.name: East New York
ChIJ81TWXQZdwokR1J1tvpkGRuo.address: East New York, Brooklyn, NY, USA

reviews.text:
787 Coffee is my absolute favorite coffee spot in NYC. The flavors are unmatched — Dulce de Leche Latte? Coquito Latte? Say less. They always have the most unique coffee blends, and every cup supports farmers and families.

The decor is cute, cozy, and low-key IG worthy: loveseats, velvet chairs, even swings.

I used to frequent the Harlem location on 129th and was so hype to find one in Brooklyn. When I walked in (clearly excited), Nettie greeted me with the warmest welcome. She answered all my questions, gave the best recommendation, and still kept everything moving behind the counter. The kindest and sweetest barista, such a gem.

As a native New Yorker — a Black American who grew up around Puerto Rican and Nuyorican friends and neighbors — the celebration of Puerto Rican culture here feels familiar. Not trendy. Familiar. Neuvayolllll.

I’ll definitely be back. And Nettie better be there too!

We came here for the weekend celebrating my fiancés birthday. I came just to try it out and loved it, I ended up coming back the next morning before our commute back home to get another coffee. Friendly staff both days, very relaxed vibe. I enjoyed it !

We found this spot by accident and ended up loving it. The coffee had great flavor, and the employee who took our order shared that they’re third-generation coffee roasters. Definitely worth the stop.

Beautiful stadium on the grounds of the US tennis center.
This is a great stadium with great seats, good concessions and great staff.
I've been here for AEW wrestling and not tennis but it's a great venue for other things than tennis as well.

Very conveniently accessible by the subway. The US Open is really well organized with a lot of freebies, experiences, lounge access & great food/drink options! All the stadiums are pretty close to each other. The Arthur Ashe is the biggest one and usually the best players play there! A great experience watching the beat tennis players in action

Arthur Ashe Stadium is the best. It's the biggest tennis stadium in the world and it feels huge, but you can still see everything. The best part is the roof. You never have to worry about rain and can watch the whole match. The atmosphere is crazy, especially during night matches. If you're going to the US Open, you have to get a ticket for Arthur Ashe.

ChIJRXwMyjSsNTERcAR9OfSTxfk.name: Yen So Park
ChIJRXwMyjSsNTERcAR9OfSTxfk.address: QL1A Công viên, Gamuda Central, Yên Sở, Hà Nội 100000, Vietnam

reviews.text: We have our Christmas Day prayer there after walking 2.4km from the hotel we stayed. The choir was amazing. So beautiful voice and the ochestra was amazingly nice. The church is beautiful and unique in colour. It's free. No need ticket

Nice looking Catholic Church. The service I attended was in Vietnamese, and it was very  well organised, and all attendees seemed very engaged. However, I was stopped by a group of older women who told me that if I wasn't a Catholic, I shouldn't be there. This made me feel very disappointed about the Catholic community in Hanoi. I have been to many holy Christian sites, including the Vatican city and Jerusalem. Not one time I was questioned and asked to leave for not being Christian. It is my understanding that all are welcome in the house of the Lord. I told them they shouldn't stop anyone from entering the Church. Unfortunately, older people in Hanoi have a lot of power, so they do not listen to someone younger than them. Having said that, the younger people seemed very friendly. The toilets are clean and free to use.

A good place to camp for the weekend. Large space, many trees,
Enjoy playing away from the hustle and bustle of Hanoi city🥰🥰
But it is not good that there is only 1 toilet, not clean,...

ChIJGVltnX6zp0wRRCLh1dsdsY0.name: Imperial Theatre
ChIJGVltnX6zp0wRRCLh1dsdsY0.address: 12 King Square S, Saint John, NB E2L 5B8, Canada

reviews.text: It was incredible. It was clean and beautiful to sit. The show was more than I could ask. It was amazing incredible I'd go again for sure

The Imperial Theatre in uptown Saint John is nothing short of a gem, and our recent experience attending "The Nutcracker" ballet truly showcased its charm and elegance. From the moment we stepped inside, we were enveloped in an atmosphere of historic grandeur and artistic vibrancy.

The theatre itself is beautifully styled, with its stunning architecture and intricate details that transport you to a different era. The plush seating and intimate layout ensure that every patron feels connected to the performance, making it a perfect venue for a ballet as beloved as "The Nutcracker." The soft glow of the chandeliers set the stage for an enchanting evening, and the acoustics are superb, allowing every note of Tchaikovsky's timeless score to resonate through the hall.

As for the performance, it was a magical experience! The dancers brought the story to life with grace and precision, captivating the audience with their skill and artistry. Each scene was a feast for the eyes, with vibrant costumes and imaginative set designs that truly captured the spirit of the holiday season. The blend of classical ballet with the festive atmosphere made for an unforgettable night.

The staff at the Imperial Theatre were warm and welcoming, enhancing our experience with their attentiveness and enthusiasm. Whether you’re a local or just visiting, catching a performance here is a must. The Imperial Theatre is not just a venue; it's a celebration of culture and the arts in Saint John. We left the theatre feeling uplifted and inspired, already looking forward to our next visit! 🌟
We visited St John and decided to check out the theatre.  The tour guides were very knowledgeable and very friendly.  This place is a well maintained treasure.  Amazing place.

Located on the lower floor of this unique multipurpose building, sits Cafe Bewilderment. One of the charms of the cafe is the decor, which is super clean and plants hanging from the ceiling. So you get a comfortable cozy space that’s perfect for socializing, and listening to live music playing in the background. The bar area is perfect if you want to enjoy one of their well made cocktails or non alcoholic drinks. The Cafe Bewilderment menu is on the small side, even smaller as the night goes on. But you can find small bites including sandwiches on there. There’s even a selection of bake goods that is well worth trying out.

Grilled cheese- loaded with the right amount of bacon jam. Had a really good salty sweet flavor profile.

Clover & sage cocktail- amazing drink

Butter tart- tasted just like pecan pie.. I could eat a dozen of these

I stopped by Café Bewilderment today for the first time and it instantly became my new go-to spot. The atmosphere is beautiful and cozy, and really welcoming.
I sat at the bar and asked the woman working there if it was okay to sit even if I wasn’t ordering alcohol, and she was so kind. She said, “Of course you can sit here! You don’t have to order food or coffee just stay as long as you like.” That immediately made me feel comfortable.

I ordered a cardamom latte and a warmed lemon blueberry scone (around $14), and both were incredible. The coffee was smooth, the flavors were perfect and rich, and the scone was delicious. I stayed for about two and a half hours to finish some work and loved every minute.

The staff were genuinely friendly and the vibes were amazing. I visit cafés often in both Toronto and Buffalo, and this one really stands out, the energy here just makes you want to slow down and stay awhile. Can’t wait to come back and try their brunch menu!
I had been wanting to check out this cafe for some time now, and it did not disappoint! Beautiful space, tasty coffee and food. Looking forward to going back one evening for cocktails!

My first time here at Fresco Cafe, and I’m extremely impressed! The Mozzarella Sticks was good, but the Hallumi Salad was out of this world, so fresh so good, I will have to come back! The mushroom Truffle Tostini is amazing, together with the sauce it is even better! The cinnamon Latte goes well together and I loved it! their pastries looks amazing! I got a chocolate halva Haman Tash it was delicious! I will be back!

Had breakfast this morning by fresco.
I’m really happy they opened. The food and vibe is awesome! We had shakshuka and American breakfast.
Food was delicious and fresh. The shakshuka was honestly one of the best ive ever had!
We were lucky and got a chocolate croissant out of the oven. It was insane. They should put up a Krispy crème style light to let people know when then they have it fresh pastries!

Fresco in Pomona, NY is a fantastic spot with an elevated menu and a cozy, modern vibe. I ordered the Mushroom Truffle Tostini and it was absolutely delicious, rich, savory, and perfectly balanced with that deep truffle flavor. What really impressed me was that it even came with a little side salad, which added a nice fresh touch to the dish. Everything was plated beautifully and tasted just as good as it looked. The service was friendly and attentive, and you can tell the kitchen takes real pride in what they serve. If you’re in Rockland County and looking for a classy yet approachable dining experience, Fresco is a great choice.

Had a great time visiting the zoo. Though it was a chilly day so few parts of the zoo were empty like the poultry area zebras, hyenas etc but we had a good luck to see these amazing and majestic animals. Polar bear and snow leopard were very comfortable due to the chilled weather and it was a treat to see them play. Recommended for children to must visit with their parents and have a fun outing.

Wonderful, small zoo with great exhibits. Some exhibits were under construction and there weren’t any animals in them. Other than that it is a great morning or afternoon to spend with nature and its animals. There is a lovely walkway around a park that is disability accessible outside the zoo as well.

Went on free Thursday in October was great weather not hot or cold was plenty of people for a weekday.  Got to see the baby red panda were so cute .We had a burger at the burger restaurant was very good weird the pop was more than the burger! Spent couple of hours there got to see everything loved the polar bear and giraffes and the rainforest.

ChIJt8_21M7cwokRLEbM7AqjBrs.name: New York Boulders
ChIJt8_21M7cwokRLEbM7AqjBrs.address: 1 Phil Tisi Wy, Pomona, NY 10970, USA

reviews.text: Such a cute baseball stadium! I am absolutely obsessed with it! Very clean! Very comfy! You have awesome views doesn’t matter where you sit. Friendly staff. The stadium food is good but there aren’t many options, however, when there are additional vendors in the stadium, you have tons of options. Always opt in for the funnel cake. You can’t go wrong. Parking rates vary as I’ve paid $5 for some games and $10 for others. Leaving the lot is a bit stressful at the end of the game as exits are limited so patience is definitely needed, however, you will not go wrong with visiting this stadium. Such a great experience. Fireworks every Saturday during the summer!

First time visiting this ballpark & it did not disappoint.  Natural grass field was nice 2 see. Not a bad seat in the park & had a huge fully stocked team store. Visited on Irish  🇮🇪 heritage nite & they had live music which was enjoyable.  Mini golf ⛳️,  corn 🌽 hole, playground  & evan a train🚞 4kids & kids at heart that went fair pole to fair pole . Plenty of ballpark food🍕🌭🍦 options & the staff was friendly & helpful.  Ballpark was off the beaten path ,which was a nice suprise. Parking 🚘was $5 & 3 tix was $53- not bad. Fireworks 🎇🎆 did not disappoint & players were happy to sign autographs.  You can also walk around the entire ballpark & take in different views. If u R a baseball fan u need 2 put this 1 on your list 4 sure. Well  that's  all I got thanks 4 reading 📚 my review  & hope this helpes 😁😎👌✌️👍⚾️🧢

The ballpark is a great local venue in Rockland County right off the parkway. They have reasonable ticket prices & plenty of promotions. We love the weekly firework displays & plenty of additional night's with fireworks/give-aways. Bottom line the place is really a dump, with broken faucets, dirty areas, weeds under the seats (I literally had a tree growing under my seat) & trash. The $10 mini golf had piles of trash, loose bricks just lying all over the place. I mean deep cleaning and some minor repairs what’s that cost? The park, the food, & the vendors are just tired and need some paint/updates. Have the cold calling ticket office guys go out and pull some weeds from under the seats. Do me a favor Clover Mgt. take a FREE ferry ride out to Staten Island and see the Ferryhawks, take notes! You guys are trying with the promos, theme nights, & free tickets but the place needs a refresh. $5 dollar parking half the lot pitch black!
Great atmosphere with a covered terrace. I had the Curry Legumes, Boeuf Bourguignon, and Norwegian Benedict. The Tiramisu Caramel was quite original as well. I highly recommend it!
Very cozy on a Tuesday evening. We ordered two teas and a portion of mozzarella sticks. Everything was delicious!

A lovely place with beautiful décor and a warm atmosphere. The staff were friendly, and although we stopped by just for a quick coffee, our visit happened to be on a Friday, which made it extra special.

There was a live band of three senior musicians performing and singing in a very charming, distinctly French style. Their performance added such a joyful and unique ambiance to the place, and it was lovely to see everyone around engaging with the music and enjoying the moment.

I later learned that this live music experience is offered at the venue on Fridays only

The coffee here is outstanding - we had a cappucino and a flat white and both were excellent. The interior is small and industrial. Staff are friendly, including the exceptionally well behaved cafe dog!

Amazing coffee spot with fresh pastries! The coffee here is truly exceptional - try their Gesha or Wush Wush for something special. If you prefer milk-based drinks, we also had flat white - wonderfully soft and smooth. So to the beautiful service, you’ve got one of the best coffee experiences around. Make sure to grab Kolo coffee beans with you to replicate this experience at home ☕

One of the best coffee shops in Paris! I’ve been here at least 5 times. & their chocolate chip peanut butter cookie is also one of my favorites. Lydia is a great barista, too, and so friendly! An easy 5/5 ⭐️

ChIJyXgSNGtx5kcRA1_dub1akkI.name: Café d’Auteur - Specialty Coffee shop & roaster

reviews.text:
I surprised why this place still doesn’t have Michelin star.
