Is it more like coffee boutique, than ordinary 3rd wave speciality coffee cafe.
They have more than 10 types of grain in 4 different categories. Even the simplest one worse attention. And collection tier is breathtaking.
Every time I am visiting Paris, I try to get into this place.
Bravo Cafe D’Auteur Team.

A lovely coffee shop with an impressive selection of coffees from around the world. I tried a latte and it was excellent, with super friendly staff.

That said, given the variety of options (and the price point), it would be helpful if the staff could provide clearer guidance on the different coffees. Having them more knowledgeable about the products would really add value for customers looking for the perfect cup—especially if you’re paying €25 for a latte, it’s nice to know which coffee pairs best.

Overall, though, I definitely recommend this place!

Very interesting coffee selection and very skillfuly baristas. We had two flat whites of the daily special and they were delicious. Be prepared for quite steep prices, depending on the coffee you go for - they stock some extremely rare varities where an expresso could be above 20€. I do appreciate the wider price range and allowing customer to choose both the coffee and the drink format they want to go for. Also coffees are served with a glass of water and theu have nice and well cleaned toilets, which is always a plus!

ChIJi-_lK9dx5kcRgWOuBWkrAnU.name: Théâtre du Vieux Colombier
ChIJi-_lK9dx5kcRgWOuBWkrAnU.address: 21 Rue du Vieux Colombier, 75006 Paris, France

reviews.text: Very conveniently located in Paris with 3 metro lines 4, 10, 12 and buses. Nice and cosy theatre with comfortable seats. One of three locations for high quality theatre performances by La Comédie Française!  Also, a great café inside offering a snack before, during entracte and after the shows.

The Théâtre du Vieux-Colombier, located in the heart of Paris’s Saint-Germain-des-Prés, is a cultural gem with a rich history and an intimate setting that makes it a standout venue for theater enthusiasts. Founded in 1913 by Jacques Copeau, a visionary who sought to break away from the commercialized, star-driven theater of his time, this 300-seat venue has been a beacon of artistic innovation for over a century. Since 1993, it has served as the second stage of the prestigious Comédie-Française, offering a platform for both classic and contemporary works.

Always a good evening. I’ve seen a lot of children’s theater here, but this time it was 100% adults for a political social criticism piece and it was (as always) done right with amazing actors and multimedia touches to get the atmosphere just right. You do need to understand French because there is no dubbing or subtitles!

An architectural marvel that is immediately striking due to its unique, mismatched towers that give the building a distinctive and memorable character. Stepping inside reveals a vast and soaring space filled with incredible treasures, including the world-renowned frescoes by Eugène Delacroix in the Chapel of the Holy Angels. The atmosphere is further enriched by one of the largest pipe organs in the world, which creates a powerful sense of wonder. Visitors can also discover the historic brass meridian line built into the floor, adding a fascinating scientific element to the spiritual beauty of the site.

This lovely church contains the gnomen scientific line and column made to measure the height of the sun, but made more recently famous by author Dan Brown mentioning its association with the rose line in his book the davinci code. The church also has many wonderful paintings and sculpture, including a whole section by Delacroix. I found the sculpture of the skeleton particularly dramatic and well executed. The square in front of the church helps lend it a grandeur.

The Church of Saint-Sulpice, located in the fashionable 6th arrondissement of Paris, is one of the most significant and largest churches in the city, second only to Notre-Dame in size. Its construction took more than 130 years, from 1646 to 1780, and involved several architects, resulting in a distinctive blend of Baroque and Classical styles. The church was originally built on the site of an older 12th-century Romanesque church.
The building's facade remains unfinished, lacking one of its planned towers, giving it a unique, somewhat asymmetrical appearance. The interior is striking in its scale and austere elegance.
The main treasures inside are the frescoes by Eugène Delacroix, located in the first chapel on the right.
Another unique artifact is the gnomon, an astronomical instrument created in the 18th century to accurately determine the date of Easter. It consists of a copper strip embedded in the floor of the nave and a system of holes in the windows that allow sunlight to fall on a white marble obelisk and a scale on its surface at noon, marking the spring equinox.

ChIJEYElfrdx5kcR8NJ6jn7CDG8.name: Café du Rendez-Vous
ChIJEYElfrdx5kcR8NJ6jn7CDG8.address: 2 Av. du Général Leclerc, 75014 Paris, France

reviews.text:
Great place with good food ! Never disappointed.
Service was very nice and clean.
Good selection of food - nothing outstanding but still very good and well plated.

A really lovely place, friendly staff, tasty food, and good prices. Some ingredients could be replaced and dishes enhanced for a better taste, but it's still really good. Looking forward to coming back! A great place to gather with friends over a good drink and a variety of food.

Before heading down in to the catacombs we came in for breakfast as it’s just across the road. €16.90 for croissant, baton, jam, butter, orange juice, coffee, omelette and side salad - that’s a lot of food for such a low price!
Friendly staff, we were moved to a bigger table so all the food could fit, tasty food with decent butter and jam for the baguette. The omelettes weren’t the best and could have done with being hotter but they were still tasty. Juice was freshly squeezed, croissants warms and the baguette was crusty.
The restaurant itself was pretty cold for some reason, maybe because they had just opened and needed to warm up and the toilets were clean and tidy.
It’s the best coffee shop in the whole Lille!
I like this new place
Great coffee, amazing (four legged) resident!

Although it is just a simple gate, i love walking in that area and admiring the architect and old historic symbol of this monument if you go very early in the morning, it will be empty, and you can take some nice pictures with the gate and the belfry in the background

It stands on the site of the former Porte des Malades (Gate of the Sick), a medieval gate that led to a nearby leper hospital. This monumental triumphal arch symbolises the historic relationship between the old Flemish rule and new French rule. As I approached this gate from Jean-Baptiste Lebas Park it invoked a sense of history. It leads me directly towards the old city centre with the Lille Mairie on the right hand side with its 104 metres UNESCO listed belfry tower. I could just made out the statues of Hercules and Mars in the niches representing strength and war. There are two angels at the very peak. Interestingly, the gate was one of the primary entrances to Lille for travellers arriving from Paris

Very nice place and monument to see, with a cute park and benches around it!
Had a nice time ☺️

ChIJ5WeY17lx5kcR3e0LR6qDXMM.name: Café Daguerre
ChIJ5WeY17lx5kcR3e0LR6qDXMM.address: 4 Av. du Général Leclerc, 75014 Paris, France

reviews.text: Returned after many years with fond memories of this little cafe when we’re were last in Paris…and what a rude shock.  Probably one of the rudest waiters I have ever encountered… just dropped the menu board on our table … when the breakfast came it was served with arrogance and indifference… I asked for a knife and he pointed to a small spoon for the jam and said ‘you have it’.  One of the worst coffee’s we have had in Paris and the croissant was doughy cold and no butter.  In French ….. ‘reste loin’ … stay away.

Our breakfast was fine. I really liked the croissants. They were super crispy and simply  delicious! We were the first visitors at the restaurant, so I cannot really comment on the service, since tables were still being prepared, and there was only one person, who was doing that, along with taking our order. We just asked him, if the omelette could be cooked a bit longer since we like it well done. He took it back and brought us a well done omelette without any discussion. He wasn't overly smiling or overly friendly. He just did what he had to do and we left energized for the day.

The food and cocktail were good. The staff could be more friendly. The restaurant needs to improve their cleaning. Negative point: they didn't accept the restaurant card (Edenred) which was really annoying.

ChIJrf5sxzRw5kcRphvCP6opVyM.name: The Liberation of Paris Museum - General Leclerc Museum - Jean Moulin Museum
ChIJrf5sxzRw5kcRphvCP6opVyM.address: 4 Av. du Colonel Henri Rol-Tanguy, 75014 Paris, France

reviews.text:
A wonderful museum. Very sobering given the subject matter but it is so important to be aware of the work of the resistance. Interesting exhibits as well as the text and films.
The tour around the underground bunker is not to be missed. You wear headsets which show people and settings in each of the rooms and they talk to you about what happened there in the days before the liberation. It is tremendous fun as well as informative. But be warned, there are 100 steps down and up again!
The staff were very friendly and helpful.
Incredibly, the admission is free, so please leave a donation.

World war greek here. I've been to WWII museums in London, Berlin, Cologne, Amsterdam. Great museum and absolutely free. Bravery shown by the resistance in Paris during occupation. Great story. Merci beaucoup. We did the bunker tour. Make sure you book the correct tour and get the right time. Best get them in advance

This is a small but engaging museum. There is much documented about the events of WW II, but this has a distinct French perspective, and obviously, as the name implies, focused on the liberation of Paris. The events leading up to and during the war are documented, as well, to set the context for the liberation.

There is also a bunker tour that you can take, which takes you down into a bunker that was used during he war. The bunker exhibits are simple compared to what's been curated above, but it is still informative and being in the bunker definitely gives a better sense of what life was like.

Staff were helpful and friendly, and entrance was free. Great for an hour or two.
Busy but lots of informational screens. Felt confident in knowing where to go and when! LOVED the water refill station. Toilette was 1€

Busiest and u can find everything here for transit to Paris.

Hall 3 have storage facility cum cloak room.

Wheelchairs friendly with escalators and elevators with emergency exits.

TGV trains are also available here in Hall 2

Busy all time .ther is no warm device inside.if it rain may you should have to cover your face
Some sercice .some bad and expansive coffee .

The food in the place was wonderful and our server was so delightful, he served us very well.
The restaurant is well located right opposite Saint Germain and  the decor is also very nice.
We went in a large group, and although the one server we had did his job well, but the place was understaffed and we had to wait long times for our food.
Their cauliflower steak in the sauce was so nice, never thought cauliflower could be made this wonderful.
The tiramisu was also wonderful.

Unfortunately I was not impressed with my experience at cafe Louise.
French onion soup was way overpriced and not great, I had much better onion soup for a fifth of the price at other restaurants in Paris. Definitely not worth 14 euros.
The cannelloni special was overall not good. Flavor was very strange and could barely eat half of the dish.
Service felt forced and fake. When I was done with my meal it took forever to get someone’s attention for the bill.
Would not recommend or return to this establishment.

What a beautiful spot in Paris right on Saint Germain! We were able to get a table by walking in on a Saturday and as a traveller after a long day of sightseeing this was so nice! It is such a pretty place with lots of great food options!

I had the traditional beef Bourginon and I thought it was one of the best plates that I had during my trip to Paris! Would recommend!

ChIJHYa_Sthx5kcR1ap0p3nLXbo.name: Church of Saint-Germain-des-Prés
ChIJHYa_Sthx5kcR1ap0p3nLXbo.address: 3 Pl. Saint-Germain des Prés, 75006 Paris, France

reviews.text:
A hidden gem of Romanesque history!

As the oldest church in Paris, this place offers a beautiful, peaceful escape from the busy neighborhood streets. The 19th-century interior frescoes are stunning, with vibrant colors and gold-starred ceilings that you won't see in many other Gothic cathedrals. It’s also the final resting place of René Descartes, which adds a fascinating layer of history.

Definitely worth a 15-minute visit if you are near Les Deux Magots or Café de Flore. Free entry and very well-maintained!

The Church of Saint-Germain-des-Prés offers a calm and solemn atmosphere that feels distinctly different from the busier tourist sites nearby. The interior is quiet and dignified, with a sense of historical weight that is easy to appreciate even without deep religious interest.
Architecturally, the church reflects its long history, and while it may not feel visually overwhelming or dramatic, it carries a restrained and serious character. The space encourages quiet reflection rather than excitement, which can be refreshing if you are looking for a peaceful pause during a walk around the neighborhood.
There is nothing particularly surprising or striking, but the overall mood is respectful and composed. It feels like a place that remains part of everyday local life rather than a purely staged attraction.
A solid stop if you are nearby, especially for those who appreciate atmosphere and history over spectacle.

Very nice church! The Abbaye de Saint-Germain-des-Prés is an absolute must-see in Paris! It’s one of the oldest churches in the city and full of charm and history. The atmosphere inside is so peaceful, with beautiful arches, paintings, and that soft light that makes everything feel timeless. You can really feel the history in every corner. It’s a quiet little escape from the busy streets of Saint-Germain. Truly a magical, inspiring place to visit!

ChIJFWApGe5u5kcRNR-Xz_NKsNc.name: Bauer Stadium
ChIJFWApGe5u5kcRNR-Xz_NKsNc.address: 92 Rue du Dr Bauer, 93400 Saint-Ouen-sur-Seine, France

reviews.text: Ground only opened on two sides. A bit difficult to find your entrance.

The atmosphere at Red Star is simply fantastic. Supporters are very inclusive and the songs are melodic and simple enough that even anglophones like me can sing along. The stadium has a long history and sadly a whole section of the stadium is non functional. But still, the Kop end, the standing tier is absolutely en fuego. Bring your singing voice and cheer the boys. It's a fantastic day out in the community!
lovely main stand..the behind the goal looks interesting... friendly staff...get in early

ChIJhUMDKpetNTER5kdrceS16tA.name: Paris Baguette Trung Hòa
ChIJhUMDKpetNTER5kdrceS16tA.address: 17T5 P. Hoàng Đạo Thúy, Trung Hoà, Thanh Xuân, Hà Nội 100000, Vietnam

reviews.text:
Clean, tidy & spacious seating area. A lot of food & drinks to select from albeit pricy.
Nice place to relax.
Daily offer of 30% discount for certain items after 6 pm.
Toilet need to be cleaned more often (rubbish bin filled up to brim).
The furniture is super dirty: stains everywhere, disgusting to sit. The pastry is good, very fresh, and the coffee is average.

Came here to have some drinks and cake for a pick me up in the afternoon and left angry and stressed out. They had a promotion to get a free cheesecake for  every bill spent above 200k. I decided to spend some more to get this deal, and was told that I was not eligible since my purchases had been registered as 2 bills. I asked if they could combine my two bills since they were consecutive orders, and were given really unhelpful attitudes from 2 of the employees. I asked what difference would it make to me how many bills they put my purchases in, since to me it was the same that i spent on one single purchase, and asked them to cancel the two orders to combine them in a single bill, to which they refused. It blew my mind thay they were willing to let a customer walk away angry because of their mistakes, instead of just giving away a mere pastry that the customer was entitled to in the first place.

ChIJrRjot2J_mQARpYi5XB0VfQw.name: Praça Paris
ChIJrRjot2J_mQARpYi5XB0VfQw.address: Av. Augusto Severo, 342 - Glória, Rio de Janeiro - RJ, 20021-040, Brazil

reviews.text:
Sept.25: we came through this park as we wanted to get to Gloriá beach.

It was very well maintained & clean with some fountains & other statues as well as very beautiful lights.

Honest traveler review:
This is a hidden place to visit in Rio, aim for a weekend as you may find vendors of local food and drinks. The area is very well organized with a good level of security and clean toilettes.
The views and place scenic for great pictures.
SQUARE ,HUGE AND WELL CARED FOR EXTREMELY RICH IN INFORMATION .GOOD LOCATION ,FREE ACCESS. GIFT SECURITY

Been to many events spaces arround the world but cant rate this location highly enough. Easy access via the underground and then security and access were great. Went to a lady gaga concert and not a bad seat in the house. Fantastic venue
Amazing arena with great sound system lights. I went there to see Lady Gag and enjoyed the show very much. It felt safe to get there and everything was managed properly.

Good size stadium with good capacity, the high grandstand seating is a challenge to get to but view is still good. Food and drink facilities were great, many around the concourse. Plenty of toilet facilities. Easy to get in and out with the spiral walkways.

ChIJh5XcCkps5kcRRuLH-FNP-tw.name: Square de la Porte de la Villette
ChIJh5XcCkps5kcRRuLH-FNP-tw.address: 22 Rue du Chemin de Fer, 75019 Paris, France

reviews.text: What can you say polite about this place without being vilified, insulted, censored? The more we are forced to be silent, the stronger the anger will be...
I like  that  place  very much because I  live near la villette. Here  big park  science  city and  many  more  located
horriiiible !!! walking dead!!!
Ok

A large, well-stocked store! Various types of food (dry and canned), treats, toys, leashes, etc. at reasonable prices. Plus, there are many discounts and promotions available through the app. The friendly staff helped me choose the right food. I highly recommend it!
I discovered amazing food there... the animal's pack is 95% turkey meat... my picky eater forgot he has anorexia

Wonderful stop for a little lunch before leaving the Montmartre area… so friendly and helpful. Very relaxing and calm and it can be seen a favorite of locals, but all were very friendly. 🤩Was so busy enjoying ourselves forgot to take pics! Enjoyed soup of the day and gravalax salmon… perfect❣️

Highly recommend this restaurant. The staff were so welcoming and attentive the whole time we were there. We enjoyed a couple of delicious cocktails before our dinner, and our dinner was also very tasty. The atmosphere was lovely and it seemed the staff knew a lot of the customers, suggesting it was a place that people came back to regularly. If I’m ever in Paris again, I will definitely be coming back here. Thank you to all the friendly staff.

This is a busy vibrant restaurant, with plenty of atmosphere. The menu is fairly extensive and reasonably priced. E.G Mojito €7, Fish & Chips €18, John Dory fish and veg €21. The food was beautifully cooked and presented. Service very good. Would definitely recommend, especially if you are staying locally. No point in going further afield!

ChIJ5YzMRNBt5kcRube978t86bo.name: Le Pavillon des Canaux
ChIJ5YzMRNBt5kcRube978t86bo.address: 39 Quai de la Loire, 75019 Paris, France

reviews.text:
Loved to chill in this place. I have spent the whole afternoon working there, and although I was alone it didn’t feel lonely. There were so many people doing their own things - reading, journaling, working, and it made me appreciate this place. It is super spacious, the drinks are decent, and I also had a sandwich which was actually really bomb!

The place is super quirky looking and has a wonderful sitting area outside by the canal as well! Definitely 10/10.
Beautiful views and super delicious food with very warm service! Check out the interesting interior as well. Ginger and elderflower beers were great. Eggplant was a highlight.

Passing by, this place seemed cozy to us, but we were very wrong. The interior is in a retro style, but in reality, it’s in a very neglected condition. We ordered food and had to wait more than 20 minutes for each of the three categories of dishes. Meanwhile, other guests outside were served long ago — they had eaten and left.

When our food finally arrived, they brought us tasteless porridge instead of rice with meat. The porridge was both undercooked and overcooked at the same time. We never got our dessert, asked for the bill, and had to wait a long time for it too. It felt more like mockery than service.

I discovered this restaurant by accident, yet the food exceeded all expectations.
I’ve visited three times in the evening, and each time the ambiance has been warm and inviting.
The chef demonstrates exceptional craftsmanship, with clear layers of flavor in every dish.
From the main courses to the desserts, everything was surprisingly impressive

Sought out a late-night dinner. Sat immediately. Local spot near the canal. A bit more pricey. Ordered the French Onion soup and burger of the day. Both very delicious. Came out fresh. Fair prices. Good and friendly service.

We had a fantastic breakfast experience at this restaurant! The breakfast menu offers a great variety of options, and the portions are generous.

Everything we ordered tasted fresh and well-prepared. The hot chocolate was rich and velvety, with just the right amount of sweetness. The fresh-squeezed orange juice was equally impressive.

Overall, the food was delicious, the service was friendly, and the atmosphere was welcoming. I’d highly recommend this spot for anyone looking to enjoy a delicious breakfast!

Well organized, good security, bathroom facilities were versatile so that lines weren't long. Could use coat hangers in bathroom cubicles. Good signage, concessions were fairly priced and good quality though there is limited selection available. I enjoyed the venue and would attend a show there again.

I attended GD's concert in September, and it was an incredible experience! He performed magnificently—his energy, charisma, and talent completely captivated the audience. The concert atmosphere was incredible, the audience responded enthusiastically, and each performance was a delight.

It was especially nice to be back in this arena—I'd already been there for Taylor Swift's concert, and now I could compare notes. Both shows left vivid memories, and GD's performance was a true celebration for fans.

Despite some organizational issues, the concert itself exceeded expectations. Most importantly, he delivered an unforgettable experience, and that was worth the effort.

Bottom line: GD is an amazing performer, and his show is worth seeing in person. I'm glad I was able to be a part of this evening!

Awesome experience, very easily accessible with roughly forty entrances. Making waiting in line a quick process. Reasonably sized with a nice atmosphere. Made my first and hopefully not last trip to Paris as memorable as I could possibly have hoped. The last time is now 🥲

ChIJEapuZOVv5kcRua-GIPTaSC0.name: Café du Marais
ChIJEapuZOVv5kcRua-GIPTaSC0.address: 3 Rue du Bourg Tibourg, 75004 Paris, France

reviews.text:
We stopped by this café on our last morning in Paris, located right in the heart of the Marais. The beautiful décor immediately caught our attention and set a lovely tone. The staff were friendly and welcoming, and the service was impressively quick. The food was good, with the savory waffles standing out as a unique and enjoyable option.
A charming place for a relaxed breakfast and a lovely way to end our trip. ☕🥐

Came here for breakfast and it was delicious! Great prices. Chose the option to get a latte, orange juice, pain au chocolate & scrambled eggs with bacon all for 15 euros. Service was kind. Cafe itself is very cute. Would recommend & would come back again.

An extremely cute café right in the heart of the Marais district. We had brunch here and ordered waffles with avocado, sunny-side-up egg, salmon, and spinach, as well as a ham and cheese sandwich. The portions are very generous – we couldn’t finish everything – and the food was very good.

The coffee isn’t specialty level, but it’s acceptable considering the kind of coffee that’s typically served in Paris. The place itself is cozy, with a touch of Selby chic. I sat inside on a rainy day and it was perfect. A very useful find in Paris – I’ll definitely come back next time I visit the city.

ChIJKcmUz1Nx5kcRXqnReft2its.name: Café abel Coffee and Brunch
ChIJKcmUz1Nx5kcRXqnReft2its.address: 2 Rue de l'Hôtel Saint-Paul, 75004 Paris, France

reviews.text:
We stopped in for breakfast on our way to the Bastille market and it was the perfect spot for a bite!

They could easily accommodate our group of 4 as the space is quite open inside. We got simple lattes but they were very tasty. The omelette and avocado toast were a particular hit!

This is a fantastic place to have breakfast in Paris. The menu is very interesting lacks a little bit of protein for breakfast, but you can still find protein. We had a great experience. The service was impeccable.

We ordered the pancakes, which were good, the chicken salad was out of this world. The vinaigrette on the salad was tremendous and it had a lot of fresh ingredients.

My family also ordered the green bowl with granola and yogurt, which was delicious.

There are not a lot of gluten-free alternatives, but they were kind enough to prepare eggs for my wife.

The almond latte and the flat white

Certainly, a recommended place to have breakfast.

I wanted a special breakfast on my last morning in Paris and Cafe Abel was perfect! It is a beautifully decorated Parisian cafe and the food was divine. I had the pancakes with melted butter and herb omelette.

ChIJ3eCHM_1x5kcRRfrf5-yoThc.name: The Caféothèque of Paris
ChIJ3eCHM_1x5kcRRfrf5-yoThc.address: 52 Rue de l'Hôtel de Ville, 75004 Paris, France

reviews.text:
This café is a real hidden gem. The coffee beans are no joke many of them come from top quality farms in Africa, and you can really taste the difference. The space is filled with plants (real ones, everywhere), and there’s plenty of seating, which makes it perfect for work meetings or remote working.

After visiting several museums, I was very selective about where to sit down and rest, and I’m glad I chose this place it didn’t disappoint at all. I’ll definitely be coming back. The owner is also very kind and welcoming, so take a moment to chat with him if you can.
Once of Paris' coffee go tos. Delicious in-house coffee and delicious pasties. Friendly, comfortable and on a sunny day the tables out the front are hard to beat.

Great coffee but not service oriented. If you like the traditional Parisian attitude of behaving to customers as if they were in a soviet-era canteen, this is your place. Otherwise, you might want to look somewhere else.
Also, long queues (twice, once for paying and then for ordering) and relatively high prices.
Still, great coffee (but there is also great coffee elsewhere).

Fantastic hotel lobby bar-cafe-restaurant at the Mercure Southbank, which is open for lunch, and dinner (from 17:30). Plus serving drinks and pastries/sandwiches in between (plus the hotel's breakfast buffet). Food exceeded all expectations - it was all excellent. I leant into the proper mains, but there were also plenty of crowdpleasing options (burger, schnitzel, pasta, fish n chips). Staff were great. Atmosphere was relaxed. It's nicely furnished, and, with plenty of light, provides a comfortable space to spend time in (aside from there being loads of TV screens). I loved it, and popping down was a highlight of my stay at the Mercure.

ChIJveP9iiBo1moRTxhTiWaGy0I.name: South Yarra Presbyterian Church
ChIJveP9iiBo1moRTxhTiWaGy0I.address: 621 Punt Rd, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia

reviews.text: Discover the Gospel of Jesus Christ, with the regulars. I'm regularly here and often and current enough to know this Google maps status designation as Temporarily Closed is WRONG. Google please make amends, I've provided photos of the car park of attenders filling up Today!

I have been welcomed , encouraged,supported,the church follows the bible and the people in the church show me real Christianity in action..I am on a journey,seeking Christ,the church people are leading me in the right way
Very good sermon which holds Bible to be God's words; harmonious and close-knit Christian community

ChIJry7j_5xC1moRJBVX8VQE7Wc.name: Darling Cafe
ChIJry7j_5xC1moRJBVX8VQE7Wc.address: 2 Darling St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia

reviews.text: Amazing food and service! The steak sandwich was so tender and cooked beautifully! The Nasi Goreng had the perfect balance of ingredients and was fantastic! Definitely recommend and I am excited to come back 😊

On a rainy Melbourne morning, this cosy café was the perfect way to brighten my day. Just a short walk from my hotel, I stumbled across this relaxed spot and was not disappointed. The service was wonderful, the early-morning ambience calm and inviting, the coffee strong, smoothy  delicious—and my breakfast was truly yummy. Highly recommend!

Absolute hidden gem off the beaten track! It actually has great tasting coffee and also food that has flavour! Thanks to Frey and Arisa (hope i didn't butcher the names) for always bringing their best selves and service with smile
Restaurant and food hub at the entrances. Other stores nearby.

Freshwater place is fine if you have a reason to be there. It has a few cheap food alternatives, you just can’t eat them outside because the birds are quite aggressive there towards anyone eating.

It looks nice enough and is close to the Yarra River and Flinders St Station. I find it easy to access, but I need a reason to be there!

I Like this residential building a lot. It has great facilities which are available on 2 different levels.
They have a very well equipped gym (new equipment), pool, spa, steam room, sauna, movie room (upgraded soon), massage rooms, large outdoor area with several BBQs, meeting room etc.

I recorded the video from level 59 penthouse and another video from one of the BBQ areas. Awesome city view just next to the river.

Excellent, wide and deep beer selection. Spent a birthday here with a group. Friendly staff provided us with a somewhat secluded corner which was great. Good food, e.g. the burger and the sausages with mashed potato. The cheese croquettes are misnamed though, they are crumbed bits of cheese and mediocre.
The outside seats allow for some great people watching.

I would give six stars if I could. So lovely staff, they are genuine and it's old school real service with smile! Great selection of Belgian beers. We also had some pretzel, fries and mussel pot. Delicious and it tasted like we were back in Europe! Highly recommended place and we will definitely go back when we are in Melbourne next time. It is pretty crowded, for a good reason, so I would recommend booking a table if you go during peak times.

Lovely atmosphere, great beer (the fruit beer is amazing!), and tasty food. The staff are really friendly and the service is excellent. Highly recommend booking a table in advance. Definitely a place I’d love to come back to!

Lovely building beautiful architecture. Seats comfy. Saw Cluedo which was hillarious with an amazing twist. Had a lovely cocktail at the bar of the theatre. They could have more seating inside the bar area.

One of the excellent smaller auditoriums.
We arrived early just to see the amenities before the show; A Christmas Carol.
Everything from bathrooms to bar staff were excellent.
Knowing a seating plan, I chose well with seats in stalls and I wasn’t disappointed.
Excellent seats and views were afforded.
Hasn’t been in years but will return more often.
Show was a hit too. We loved it
Staff were so supportive for my mum's wheelchair access. They deserve more than 5 stars.

ChIJQXsLmTFD1moRy4IlHdYzWKo.name: AMIRI CAFE
ChIJQXsLmTFD1moRy4IlHdYzWKo.address: Shop R02, 14 Cnr Lonsdale Street, QV, Melbourne VIC 3000, Australia

reviews.text: The restaurant is spacious, has a nice atmosphere, and is busy. Customers order food via QR code but pay at the counter. The food doesn't take long to arrive, looks good, and tastes delicious, but the prices are a bit high.

I met my friend here for brunch. Nice location and good vibe.
QR code to order, but pay at the register when leaving.
My meal choice was the Kyoto Omurice. I’m not sure it was a great selection for brunch. I found it quite heavy on the stomach. The burger was substantial, but it was just a lump of meat with no flavour. There was plenty of sauce, but it was lukewarm. Overall, this dish lacked flavour, but surprisingly it’s labelled as ‘popular’.
On the other hand, my friend’s dish, Fried Chicken HotCakes was very good and full of flavour. Side note, there was only one hotcake (not hotcakes).
Staff were friendly and attentive.

highly recommend, one of the best cafes i have visited in melbourne cbd!! the overall appearance is super instagram worthy, staff are friendly and helpful, ideal date spot, tried the cake, drinks, and some food all was really great!! also the music in store was really nice lol:) i went with one other person and our total was around $60 for a brunch meal. something you may want to know: there is weekend surcharge and card surcharge, but there is a 5% discount if you pay by cash. overall i love this place, will definitely go again!!!

Cool-down on a scorcher! This is the perfect remedy for a 41.1°C Melbourne day. We stopped in for the Yuzu Matcha bingsu and iced lattes. They were exactly what we needed... refreshing, cooling, sweet... absolutely hit the spot.
​It is on the pricier side for bingsu but you can taste the quality ingredients, so it is delicious nonetheless.
The service was quick and efficient with no wait time.
I love the bingsu here. The quantity is good enough for 2 people. It’s usually busy around evenings so we have to wait in queue outside to get a table. They have good flavour options as well!

I had an amazing visit at Sulbing, and it easily became one of my favorite dessert spots in Melbourne. I ordered the pistachio chocolate bingsu, and it was incredible 💕 rich pistachio spread, smooth chocolate sauce, crunchy kadaif, and soft, fluffy shaved ice that all came together perfectly.

I also tried the strawberry hotteok, which ended up being my personal favorite. The warm, chewy hotteok with the bright strawberry filling was such a good balance.

For drinks, I had the ice Biscoff latte and the injeolmi latte. Both were sweet, comforting, and great alongside the desserts.

Plus, the café stays open till late, which makes it an easy go-to for night hangouts or a late dessert craving.

Highly recommend if you’re looking for delicious Korean desserts! 🍧✨

ChIJH4H-YZhC1moReYT-UlrpsOQ.name: Melbourne Girls Grammar
ChIJH4H-YZhC1moReYT-UlrpsOQ.address: 86 Anderson St, South Yarra VIC 3141, Australia

reviews.text: Absolutely loved the peanut butter French toast! It was the perfect mix of sweet and salty. The simple toppings of butter and condensed milk soaking into the pillowy soft bread was heavenly. At only $8 for a giant serve it was great value too. We also tried the crispy noodles with mixed vegetables which was really tasty and loved the texture of the noodles. Serving sizes were very generous and we couldn’t finish it all but really enjoyed what we had. The options for vegetarians are quite limited but there are a few meals and snacks available. The drinks menu is quite extensive and includes some traditional Hong Kong style offerings and some nostalgic treats like Ovaltine and Ribena. The golden brick French toast and some of noodle dishes we saw other diners having looked incredible.  Service was fast and friendly with the option to order via QR code or at the counter. We’ll definitely be back for more of that PB French toast!

An authentic Hong Kong–style restaurant with delicious food and a very wide variety of dishes. There are so many choices that you could come here many times and still not get bored.

The environment is clean and well maintained. Hot and cold drinking water, as well as sharing bowls, are available for customers to help themselves.

The most memorable feature is the restroom. The owner designed an iPhone-style passcode lock graphic outside the restroom door, which almost makes you think a password is required to enter! It’s a fun and clever touch that really brings a smile to your face.

SINCHAN or me eating Golden brick beef curry? 🤯🔥

Real Hong Kong flavours done properly🥢 from classic roast meats to silky rice rolls soaked in hoisin & sesame.
Spam fries, pineapple bun, fish siu mai… not a single miss.

If you love HK food, this one’s a must. 🇭🇰✨

Ig: worldofnom.au

Tried their eggs Benedict and it was absolutely delicious! Eggs poached well and hollandaise light and creamy. The mocha was nice too though could've been warmer. Staff were pretty efficient despite the busyness. Always love the atmosphere of degraves St, makes me feel like I'm on holiday visiting.

Price guide (as of Oct 2025):
Benedict $25
Mocha $6.50

I often come here for lunch and they always welcome me in such a lovely and special way!
The pasta is absolutely delicious❤️ My favorite is the Chilli Prawn.
I usually get it with either olive oil or tomato base, and the prawns are always so fresh.
The balance between the sauce and pasta is just perfect, and they even give you Parmesan cheese if you ask.
I also love their coffee
honestly, the best coffee in Melbourne! ☕💛

True to Melbourne‘s coffee scene, this cafe is a must visit for Sydney people. We don’t have the same vibe in the heart of Sydney, a real authentic laneway hideout feel to this cafe. Food is good and service is good.

ChIJh0lVrq1C1moRAH6d5TRhrLY.name: Donor Tissue Bank Of Victoria
ChIJh0lVrq1C1moRAH6d5TRhrLY.address: Coroners Courts, 65 Kavanagh St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text: Small organisation, but mighty in power when it comes to serving the community at large. Great place to work with, under the umbrella or Victorian Institute of Forensic Medicine.

ChIJzahLYf5D1moRrem1Q4hSaAA.name: Misty Pot Korean Grill Bar & Cafe
ChIJzahLYf5D1moRrem1Q4hSaAA.address: 35 Dryburgh St, West Melbourne VIC 3003, Australia

reviews.text: This little gem of a restaurant tucked away in West Melbourne was a delightful suprise. My partner and I had lunch here and the service/food was absolutely delicious. The restaurant sits about 25 pax and has a cozy atmosphere. My partner ordered a noodle dish and I ordered the pork. Both were flavoursome and beautifully present. We will definitely be back to try some other dishes.

Very small cozy Korean Grill Bar & Cafe, very welcoming and friendly environment. Simple and delicious menu with some amazing chefs special to choose. Tried their La Galbi special and it was delicious. I do recommend them and it's a must visit place. I definitely be going back to try other items on the menu.

Really love this place. The food is so hearty and fulfilling. I am so grateful to have a Korean restaurant that serves amazing food with a kind smile on their face. The staff are attentive and the restaurant itself is welcoming, even if it's small.

The cemetery is divided by religious denomination, as clearly labelled on the site boards. At the main entrance is a memorial garden for Australian Prime Ministers, a lovely rose garden, and a new mausoleum with a reflective glass facade. Here you will also find toilets and the office.
There is no extravagant statuary such as you often find in European city cemeteries and large areas are densely crowded with graves with no grass or trees. The gravestone inscriptions often indicate family origins in England, Scotland, Wales and Ireland which will be of interest to family historians.

A vast cemetery and place of interest in the middle of Melbourne.  Beautifully maintained gardens and graves.  Plenty of room to walk through and read on some of the fascinating graves and headstones.  Despite being surrounded by a city population, you can find comfort and have a quiet time to reflect whilst on the grounds.

Beautifully maintained cemetery, certainly the largest I’ve ever been to. If you come through the main entrance there’s an office where you can pick up a detailed map, the wonderful staff can also help you with grave location, and there’s even umbrella’s you can borrow which was a lifesaver on the warm day we came. There’s toilets and water fountains spread throughout.
We spent no less than 3-4 hours here and barely scratched the surface.
A nice relaxing place for a short walk near the water .
Good place
To experience

ChIJfV4IPhF-bIcRi-EeF3_nGlg.name: Saint Mary’s Anglican Catholic Church
ChIJfV4IPhF-bIcRi-EeF3_nGlg.address: 2290 S Clayton St, Denver, CO 80210, USA

reviews.text: This is where my journey began, i was destined to cone here and serve HIM for rest of my life. Father Bell, Decn. Ryan held my hand and guided me to find purpose of my life. The masses, fellowship always make me feel rooted to that purpose. This is a great homely place of worship where we we can pray and spend a moment of solitude and go out recharged. I highly recommend visiting the Church and definitely the people for great fun & fellowship

My husband and I first walked into St Mary's nearly 3 years ago while we were unchurched and desperately seeking traditionalism and spiritual guidance. We left our first Mass speechless, both of us with a sense that something true and beautiful was happening at St Mary's, and knowing that we wanted to be part of it.
The liturgy follows the 1928 Book of Common Prayer and the parish maintains an unapologetic dedication to traditional values -- a refreshing characteristic in a time and place where many institutions roll with the tide of popular culture.
The community within and around St Mary's has proven to be immeasurably beneficial for raising our three daughters (all under 4 y.o.) and teaching them that church isn't just "a thing we do on Sunday's," rather it is the focusing agent in our lives.
Over the last year, St Mary's has seen encouraging growth, especially among young families.

I have attended St. Mary’s for over 25 years. Actually, if you include the first  time I attended St. Mary ‘s, it would be around 30 years.  As a child, my family attended St. Mary’s when it was an Episcopal church about three blocks from our house. Then my father got transferred to Michigan but I returned to Denver to attend college and stayed here.  After college, I attended several Episcopal churches, the last one for over 20 years.  I became upset with how the Episcopal church was becoming less and less “traditional”. In trying several different churches after I left the Episcopal church, I decided to try the church of my childhood as it had become an Anglican Catholic Church.  I felt that I had come home.

ChIJ0fpagsClNTERmqYwEXHTqls.name: Trung Tâm Phân Phối Và Bảo Hành Sơn Myers

reviews.text:
To whom it may concern,

I’m writing as an Australian taxpayer who has, until now, assumed the ABC existed to serve all Australians — not to lecture, divide, or quietly drift away from the principles it claims to uphold.

What concerns me most is not a single program or presenter, but the systemic failure of governance that allows this organisation to continue on its current path while senior leadership is rewarded with remuneration reportedly in the order of $1.5 million per year.

That figure alone should demand excellence, balance, and restraint. Instead, what many Australians are seeing is content that feels increasingly ideological, racially charged, openly hostile toward allied nations such as the United States, and consistently sympathetic to one side of highly complex international conflicts — all while fuelling division at home.

The irony is hard to ignore.

The ABC regularly positions itself as a moral and intellectual authority, yet appears unwilling to apply the same scrutiny inward — particularly to its board and executive leadership, who ultimately set the tone, priorities, and culture of what is commissioned, promoted, and defended.

This is not a call for censorship.
It is a call for accountability.

If the ABC truly values independence and trust, then the board — not just the Managing Director — should be asking itself some uncomfortable questions:
•	Why does so much content feel narrow rather than representative?
•	Why do so many Australians feel spoken at instead of spoken for?
•	And why is there such a disconnect between executive pay and public confidence?

Australians are not stupid.
We notice patterns.
We notice omissions.
And increasingly, we notice when an organisation funded by the public no longer seems answerable to it.

If this feedback makes those responsible uncomfortable, that may be a sign it has landed exactly where it should.

Through the kind arrangement of one of the producers at ABC, our  Fusion Culture Group was granted a "tour" of the  ground floor hall  of the ABC Ultimo building to see our 12 meter x 13 meter collaborative food mural.  It was chosen for a solo exhibition for the month of  November,  and then another month in their Parramata premises,  but it did not eventuate for some unknown reason.  Nonetheless, our November visit was fun, educational, and meaningful.  First, our non-English speaking seniors  learned  to do digital check-in in English for entering the building, and they did it successfully.
Second, everybody was excited and happy to be able to take photos in front of the 7 pm live ABC News broadcasting studio, which was a rare opportunity. Third, we saw our FCG Mural being showcased  in a beautiful  space, especially created for it.  It made us all feel proud and a sense of achievement.  Our FCG Mural was created together by the  seniors over three art workshops led by a uni art student in July/August.
We are grateful to ABC for showcasing our FCG Mural and for providing us the opportunity to visit its Ultimo premises. The  photos and video clips shared show that our short visit was well received and very much appreciated by the seniors.
We hope that ABC will continue  its community involvement and support for a good cause. We look forward to future collaborations for mutual benefits and support.
Thank you, ABC!

Sylvia
aka Little Frog
Founder and Director
Fusion Culturel Group
9 December, 2024

A B C has been great for many years especially the gardening program. Recently though the section on individual gardens near end of program has not been focused on individual gardens but more on the individual themselves. We need to bring back the focus on gardening not individuals life story. If one wants to see individual life then "who do you think you are" is the program they should be on.
Thanks
Trev

ChIJs-Q2IlNd1moR4UGlA6wroOE.name: Baci
ChIJs-Q2IlNd1moR4UGlA6wroOE.address: Crown Riverwalk, Crown Melbourne, 21/8 Whiteman St, Southbank VIC 3006, Australia

reviews.text: Stopped here for a late breakfast on a brief visit to the Crown precinct.  Coffee was great and the omelette delicious.  Convenient location within the Crown complex - offers indoor & outdoor seating alongside the Yarra Riverwalk.  Option to order and pay via my mobile device was very convenient.  Coffee and food didn’t take long to come out.  This cafe is a good option if you want to avoid the noise & chaos of the foodcourt area.
Great service, fast service time, atmosphere is nice with indoor or outdoor seating. The food was delicious and reasonably priced. We sat in a booth which was comfortable.

The location by the river at the Crown Hotel is lovely. The staff member who served us was kind and attentive, and the coffee was very good. Unfortunately, the cleanliness let the place down: the tables looked dirty and the floor did not appear clean either.

Had brunch at Brick Lane and really enjoyed it.
The corn waffle was crispy on the outside, soft inside, with a nice natural sweetness — not heavy at all. The omelette was light, fluffy, and cooked perfectly. Simple menu items but done really well.

What stood out the most was the staff. Super friendly, energetic, and you can tell they work really well together. Even though it was busy, service was fast and everyone stayed cheerful, which made the whole place feel lively and welcoming.

Great vibe, good food, and a solid team. Would happily come back for brunch again💕

Brick Lane Melbourne

🔹 Atmosphere
Located down a small laneway near the main road, you need to walk in to find it, so it might take a moment to spot. It’s about a 7 minute walk from the State Library of Victoria, which makes it quite convenient. The indoor seating is slightly crowded, but the overall vibe is cosy. The restroom is fairly clean.

🔷 Food

🔹 Bacon & Gluten Free Toast with Avocado
I swapped the waffle for gluten free toast with avocado. The bacon was very good, cooked just right. The blueberry sauce was also delicious. The sweetness of the blueberries and the creaminess of the avocado balanced the saltiness of the bacon nicely. Together with the toast, it was a well balanced combination.

🔹 Big Brekkie Platter with Cauliflower Rice
I added cauliflower rice to the big brekkie platter. Overall, it was quite good and satisfying.

🔹 Chocolate Milk
A classic chocolate milk, smooth and rich in flavour.

🔸 Summary
A breakfast spot I would return to. So far, what I’ve ordered has been a safe choice. You’re unlikely to go wrong here

A perfect fit into the eclectic Guildford Lane of Melbourne, this cafe offers a yummy menu in an excellent ambience. It is perfect for a team lunch on a work day or a weekend brunch outing. We were lucky to get a table on a Wednesday at 12:45 pm but we could see spaces fill up very quickly, so bookings are recommended if you are a big group. The comedian/actor Dilruk Jayasinghe came to eat at the same place so we know it attracts famous people as well! haha
There's outside seating available as well, for single diners and small groups.
The open kitchen adds to the open vibe of the place. There's choctops available too to fulfil your sweet tooth cravings!
It was the first time for me eating savoury waffles and omg they were so good! They are happy to eliminate the poached eggs from the dish if you want to. My friend got the smashed avo which was great as well!

A lot of memories on flagstaff station ✨️

Little history:
Flagstaff Station, opened in 1985, is part of Melbourne’s City Loop and serves the western end of the CBD. Named after nearby Flagstaff Gardens, the station was Melbourne’s last underground station built. It plays a key role in connecting the city's bustling business district.
Pretty old antique no charging cable or top shelf machine even cost of mkyi 300$+ boggles mind when other states have free transport
Wooden framing looks amazing for photos 😅 great place right in the heart of the city

ChIJ8xA6hdNC1moRiYUJvAuyh7Y.name: The Royal Dental Hospital of Melbourne

reviews.text:
The emergency clinic is an absolute God send.

Understandably the hold time when calling is long but it is a much needed service. I was given an appointment within two days.

All staff i interacted with were so patient and kind, especially when I couldn't think straight with my tooth pain.

I have a shocking memory when it comes to names but my dentist and his assistant were amazing. Great bedside manner, knowledgeable and patient. My tooth extraction went incredibly well.

I can't give this clinic enough praise.

The parking garages were completely full but was able to find on-street parking relatively easily. 2 hours for $8. There is also a tram stop not far from the entry.

I had an excellent experience  with doctor Laura Raguine, at Royal Dental Hospital. Have re-done my root canal. Laura was professional, extremely kind , and took the time to explain everything clearly, which made me feel comfortable and confident throughout each my visit. The clinic was clean,and very well organised. The staff were friendly and welcoming from start to finish. I truly appreciate the care and attention I received and would highly recommend doctor Laura to anyone. Her kindness and service definitely 10/10. Thank you for everything doctor Laura.

To the Doctor, Nurse, and Staff at the Royal Dental Hospital,I would like to sincerely thank you all for the care and kindness you showed me during my recent visit. I had been in significant pain for several days, and your professionalism, compassion, and reassurance made a difficult experience much easier. I truly appreciate the way the extraction was handled and the support I received throughout. Thank you for the excellent care and for the important work you do every day.

ChIJseLBBeurNTERUHBT0wbEc9U.name: Government Guest House (Tonkin Palace)
ChIJseLBBeurNTERUHBT0wbEc9U.address: 12 P. Ngô Quyền, Tràng Tiền, Hoàn Kiếm, Hà Nội, Vietnam

reviews.text: Palace is under reconstruction at the moment, but looks nice

You can venture around the old-quarter and Hoan Kiem quite easily from this place. FYI, there is an imcredible coffee house/ restaurant and a historical landmark nearby. The old French building symbolizes the independence of the colony of Vietnam in 1945.

The State Guest House (Vietnamese: Nhà khách Chính phủ) is a historical monument in Hoàn Kiếm District, Hanoi, Vietnam. It is presently used as a state guest house.

The building is representative of French Colonial architecture in French Indochina. It formerly was the residential palace of the Tonkin Governor (French: Le Palais du Résident Supérieur du Tonkin, Vietnamese: Dinh Thống Sứ Bắc Kỳ), built between 1918 and 1919 to house the French Governor of Tonkin. It was later renamed the Tonkin Palace (Vietnamese: Bắc Bộ Phủ) when the Viet Minh took over northern Vietnam.

ChIJTTzjGuaNGGARRdGc3fe9gnE.name: カフェ ブラームス cafe BRAHMS

reviews.text: One of the best financiers i have ever eaten . Crispy outside and soft inside with a unique taste. Coffee was also good and well balanced.

I stopped by while wandering around Harajuku because the store had a charming atmosphere and I wanted to support a newly opened business.

I ordered the Neapolitan pasta set with drink, soup, salad, and dessert for 2500¥ (~$16 usd). The pasta was delicious, very chewy and flavorful, but the salad was a bit sad... I forgot to take a picture of my salad but it was about 6 pieces of lettuce maybe and some shredded carrot and beetroot(?). The onion soup was good. I received panna cotta as the dessert. It was tasty, but I thought the portion was a bit small. I also got an oat milk latte, but it was my first time ever having a latte, so I have no valid opinion. It certainly looked beautiful.

The atmosphere truly is lovely and the staff are really nice with good service. It was a relaxing evening dining experience with good ambience. I would want go here again next time I'm in Harajuku just for the pasta.
Nice place to relax. Food and drinks are fine, just wish they listed everything on the menu. (I see people ordering things that aren't on there, like specialty dessert drinks).
Small dedicated lovely temple! Internet said it is famous for praying for love. Very closed to Roppongi station, 3 mins walk! It is up 1 stair. Be aware not to overwalk.
Small shrine in the middle of the cityscape for blessings for relationships
small location and entrance existing quietly, please do not forget four claps

Awesome experience! The intro at the beginning was very useful, it really taught me a lot! Disclaimer, it's entirely in Japanese - so I recommend attending it only if you have a high proficiency. As for the performances, truly outstanding! For that part, subs in ENG and JP are available for free, included in the price of the ticket. It was my first live Nō experience and I believe that anyone who's ever studied it / read about it has to give it a try, seeing it live is an entirely different thing.
Great experience worth to visit. You don’t really need sit next to the stage to enjoy it. Everything is close. English subs included

One of the oldest forms of Japanese traditional performance. If you don’t mind the whole thing is all in Japanese and the pace of the performance is very slow which can probably make some audiences sleepy, you can admire the graciousness and the beauty of the Japanese culture through this show. The whole theater and stage facilities are also fantastic and very well maintained. Make sure to check the program schedule on their website in advance because the show doesn’t run every day and the showtime varies.

ChIJuS2Qp5aNGGARQLYCfvMbZW0.name: GOOD MORNING CAFE NOWADAYS
ChIJuS2Qp5aNGGARQLYCfvMbZW0.address: Japan, 〒160-0015 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Daikyōchō, 31−４ Brillia ist 千駄ヶ谷 1F

reviews.text:
Western style cafe with a Japanese touch for lunch and dinner. The staff was friendly and professional, the food servings were modest but full of flavour.
I enjoyed the salad the most.

Cute open concept cafe where we got a blend of Japanese and western breakfast. Order by QR code limited menu unlimited coffee or tea with meal. Free wifi. Was a great place to plan the day with next stop the gardens next door

Bad morning cafe
Google reviews, out of respect for actual restaurants, this place shouldn't be allowed to use the label. From start to finish, absolutely terrible.

We walked into an empty cafe and were met with a silent stare from a staffer who didn't even acknowledge us. We seated ourselves and were eventually met by a waiter we'll call Superman, who was oozing a terrible attitude. After ordering via their app, Superman returned with "over easy" eggs that were fried into dark-brown cinder blocks. When I asked for a redo, he argued that "that's how we cook them" and shot me a look like he wanted to start a fight. He then gossiped with the server, Wonderwoman, who joined in by shooting us disapproving looks.

The second egg was barely better, the bacon wasn't crispy, and the sausages were shriveled with "old-age" wrinkles. Then the manager, Napoleon, arrived to greet a VIP at the table next to us. Despite empty tables everywhere, he squeezed between us, his rear partially hanging over our table and cutlery. While the VIPs got foam art lattes, my wife’s identical order was served plain.

As we finished our tasteless meal, Napoleon returned with a coffee carafe. I predicted to my wife we’d get no refill, and I was right. Napoleon wedged his frame between the tables again to serve the VIPs, ignoring our empty cups. I honestly wondered if he had an inflatable device in those pants or was just naturally blessed with huge glutes. He finally unwedged himself and left without a word. I kept waiting for him to turn back to us and yell, "NO COFFEE FOR YOU!"

At the register, Napoleon took our money and offered a backhanded apology: "I'm sorry you don't like the way we cook our eggs." It was likely the first time he’s had to speak to a customer since earning his too-small manager vest and specially reinforced pants. Save your money; this place is a joke.

ChIJKc77NJ2LGGARIaQdb-CsP9U.name: SUZU CAFE Roppongi
ChIJKc77NJ2LGGARIaQdb-CsP9U.address: Japan, 〒106-0032 Tokyo, Minato City, Roppongi, 3-chōme−10−１ 相鉄フレッサイン

reviews.text: The buffet we had on sunday morning was very fulfilling. The scrambled eggs were excellent and the croissants were very light and flaky. The Apple juice tasted like real apples and the berries were so so good in my yoghurt

Came here on our first day in Japan for breakfast. The buffet was laid out nicely and had several choices. Tried a little bit of everything. I would recommend staying away from the scrambled eggs. They were undercooked and cold, but I tried them anyways. Ended up with food poisoning and was very ill. Three days later and I’m finally back on track overall not a bad place and everything else was good.

We got the Matcha pudding, strawberry tart and matcha! The selling point is their matcha milk, it is delicious! Matcha pudding is more bitter and has a strong taste! I think the tart was ok. Our total for 2 matcha milk, 1 tart and 1 pudding was 3,400 yen

ChIJCUP9NSnzGGARTzyUo5fQtfU.name: cafe 明日も発酵ごはん nippon

reviews.text: I had a wonderful dining experience here! The food was beautiful and their lunch set is very yummy. I had the curry set that came with a side salad. They also kindly shared with me their kombucha which was very tasty! I will be returning and I highly recommend. The lady at the store was also very kind

We had an incredibly delicious lunch here at cafe nippon. The curry and the salad set lunch tasted fantastic and were highly creative, wonderfully healthy and beautifully presented. We love fermented foods and it was so interesting to see all the different types of food that can be fermented and taste amazing. Everyone at the cafe was also so friendly and welcoming, taking time to explain the ingredients which we appreciate so much : ) cannot reccommend highly enough!

So happy we stumbled upon this hidden gem. We were warmly greeted with a cup of tea, which set the tone right away. Four of us ordered the course meal, along with the sake sampler and a few additional drinks, everything was absolutely delicious. What really stood out was how thoughtful and healthy the entire meal felt, even down to dessert. A lovely touch was the complimentary tea that kept flowing while we relaxed and enjoyed our time. Such a special, calming experience.

In short, really enjoyed this place. Clean and airy Japanese-influenced design, good quality food and ingredients with fantastic fresh fruits, while pricing/portions were reasonable by Tokyo standards. Being a Brit (albeit a long-time Japan resident), I still find the fruit/cream sandwich concept a little hard to swallow - but these were delicious.

Access by public transport is a little tricky as it’s not near a station (requiring a taxi or bus), but by car it’s straightforward with free parking. There was a queue to get in even at 8am on a Sunday but it moved quickly once it opened.

Would it be worth a journey from central Tokyo as a tourist? I would say yes, just about, as it provides a good Japan experience (high-quality fruit sandwiches! Huge parfaits!) in a different part of the city. The current weak yen also makes the prices good value.

Kajitsuya Cafe truly shines with its specialty fruit sandwiches and parfaits, each bursting with fresh, local and seasonal flavors. This café, known for its clean and spacious setting, offers both comfortable indoor and charming outdoor seating, perfect for any weather. Their menu delights not only with these fruity favorites but also with scrumptious French toast and fresh salads.

For beverages, you're well taken care of with a variety of tea options, robust coffee, and crystal-clear citrus fresh water. Since Kajitsuya is quite the popular spot, arriving early is a smart move to snag a good seat and take your time enjoying their standout offerings. Whether you're in for a quick coffee or a leisurely brunch, Kajitsuya Cafe provides a delightful culinary escape with every visit.

A great place to visit for your morning cafe. You can get a full breakfast (bread, omelets and salad) for less than 10 dollars (1200 yen). The food there was amazing, as well as the service considering how busy they usually are.

Great little cafe bakery. Enjoyed a nice Thai style lunch here. Decor is beautiful, food is tasty and service is friendly. Would recommend and come back. Baked sweets look good, but didn’t try them this time.

Cards & digital payments available.

Bike parking is available, so could be a nice stop when cycling by the Tamagawa.
A nice cafe not so far from Tamagawa. It’s good stop when you’re cycling in Tamagawa. The food is tasty and staff is friendly. I would definitely visit again.
Had a 2nd breakfast after riding along the river. Coffee, bread and sallad, delicious.

First time visiting a racecourse in Japan! The racecourse is amazingly big with food stalls selling. I'm impressed the atmosphere there and the staff were so nice to teach me how to  buy one round before the race started! Although I couldn't win a game, I enjoyed a lot there with the sincere explanation from the staff and the atmosphere (yeah, everyone so concentrated 😂). The racecourse is very very big and it takes 20 minutes walk to the museum 🐎

A truly spectacular racecourse to visit. The museum is amazing with all the past champions on show,the turfy shops , food and drink plus the ambience was amazing, even on a listed race day. The entrance fee of 200Yen is quite remarkable compared to Australia and casual attire makes it a perfect day out for all.

A great place to experience horse racing.  You will see families with babies and children coming here as weekend excursion/outing. This place is family friendly, you will be surprised! Lotsnof food outlets here too. Spacious, great for picnic too!

ChIJMyxZArn0GGAReyFnMTXDFek.name: Tokyo Gakugei University Senior High School

ChIJtbzWn2iMGGARXfmTC-Cl2Rs.name: Tokyo Kasei Gakuin Junior & Senior High School
ChIJtbzWn2iMGGARXfmTC-Cl2Rs.address: 22番地 Sanbanchō, Chiyoda City, Tokyo 102-0075, Japan

ChIJczVVxMONGGAR6v_2wlVrVB4.name: Poseidon Akasaka Cafe & Shisha Bar Lounge/ポセイドン赤坂見附シーシャ
ChIJczVVxMONGGAR6v_2wlVrVB4.address: Japan, 〒107-0052 Tokyo, Minato City, Akasaka, 3-chōme−9−４ 赤坂扇やビル 3F

reviews.text:
The first thing you notice here is that the staff are extremely friendly and very welcoming.

The environment and designs are very nice and aquatic, which suits the shop name.

The shisha recommendation by the staff was really good.

Will definitely come back on my next trips !

I came here alone after midnight during my vacation to Tokyo. The vibe was absolutely wonderful. Loved the dim, ambient lighting, chill music, and the blue fish tank. This place was perfect to relax alone for a few hours and enjoy shisha and drinks. I will definitely return next time I visit Japan.

Great Shisha Bar in Akasaka Mitsuke. The ambiente is super relaxed, the big aquarium is awesome! Shisha was good, as well as the drinks we had. My recommendation: try the unsweetened ice tea if you want to have something without alcohol. We will for sure come back next time were here! :)

ChIJOZ_4EwCNGGAR7BxJTkOswEY.name: Muffin&Bowls cafe CUPS 表参道店｜かき氷&マフィン
ChIJOZ_4EwCNGGAR7BxJTkOswEY.address: Japan, 〒150-0001 Tokyo, Shibuya, Jingūmae, 3-chōme−5−１ 神宮前３５１ビル 1F

reviews.text: The strawberry condensed milk smoothie was delicious! The staff were also very friendly and helpful! Excellent service.

A popular shop from Machida has opened its second location in Omotesando! 🥳
The spacious interior allows strollers to enter easily!
There were a few steps at the entrance, but my daughter was asleep so they helped us out. 🥹💦

When it was brought out, it was so cute, I was thrilled! 🫶🏻
And the portion size was huge!!

The regular and mini sizes were only 200 yen apart, so I chose the regular size! 🤭❣️

Luckily, they had white strawberries in stock! 🍓🤍

The pie was homemade!! Super crispy! ♡♡
And there was even pie inside the shaved ice! Surprisingly, it wasn't soggy and remained crispy. 🤤
There were also raisins inside, which added a nice texture. 😋

Overall, it was topped with a diplomate cream, a sauce that tasted like custard cream, so it really felt like I was eating a Napoleon pie!!

The ice was so fluffy, and even with this large portion, I was able to enjoy it deliciously until the very last bite! 🤗

The thoughtful gesture of offering warm hojicha tea to accompany the shaved ice was wonderful! 😍✨

There are four types of cream soda.

This time I chose the blue cream soda.

The color is so cute! 🩵

• Napoleon Pie Regular Size (¥2860)
• Blue Cream Soda (¥880)


Powdered sugar
Fresh strawberries
Diplomat cream
Caramelized bacon
Roasted almonds
Strawberry syrup
Anglaise sauce
Three kinds of raisins
Crispy pie crust
Cut strawberries
Homemade condensed milk

I went to @cups_omotesando, a stylish cafe where you can enjoy shaved ice and freshly baked muffins!

Here's what I ordered:
* Shaved Ice - Napoleon Pie (Regular) ¥2,860
* Blue Cream Soda ¥880

You can choose from four types of shaved ice (Napoleon Pie / Sakura Mont Blanc and Almond Tofu / Sakura Chocolate Mint / Strawberry S'mores and Milk Tea)!
This time I chose Napoleon Pie!
Here's what it consisted of:
* Powdered sugar
* Fresh strawberries
* Diplomat cream
* Caramelized crust
* Roasted almonds
* Strawberry syrup
* Anglaise sauce
* Three kinds of raisins
* Crispy crust
* Sliced ​​strawberries
* Homemade condensed milk

The regular size is huge! Eating it alone leaves you feeling as satisfied as if you'd eaten a whole cake! The balance of sweetness and tartness was exquisite, with fresh strawberries (white strawberries are also available this time of year!), crispy pie crust, cream, strawberry syrup, and condensed milk!

The shaved ice was filled with plenty of raisins and pie crust, allowing you to enjoy the changing textures and flavors!

The ice was incredibly fluffy and melted instantly in your mouth!

It had a gentle and light texture!

There are five types of cream soda!

This time I chose the blue cream soda!

The beautiful blue color makes it perfect for photos!

It tasted like cider!

The vanilla ice cream on top was large, smooth, and absolutely delicious!

I was able to relax and enjoy a special space in a stylish and calming atmosphere!

It's perfect for the upcoming hot season!

I highly recommend you all go and try it! ✨

The Meiji Jingu Baseball Stadium is very old. It has a lot of character. The food here is amazing. So many choices and very affordable compare to a US sporting event. For example, I was a 49ers game this year and beers were $19.50 compared to $6 beers here. We had tickets along the first base line on row 22. They were only around $60 USD for a rivalry baseball game, Tokyo Swallows vs Tokyo Giants. We had a lot of popcorn, hot dogs and noodles. They were only around $7-10 USD. The cherry on top was that they showed us on the big jumbotron.

There is a smoking area by Gate 7.

The stadium itself is well kept and easy to navigate. There’s food and souvenir options on the perimeter of the stadium, as well as some food options inside. Brought a backpack to store my stuff and didn’t have any issues. It was a great experience!! Don’t forget your umbrella!! We are the Swallows!!

I'd say this stadium has a very different feel with Tokyo Dome. Watching a baseball game here was fun and a new experience for me since I'm usually at the Tokyo Dome, which is a closed stadium.

Anyway, the vibe sure is different.
I'm just thankful that the weather on the day we watched the game was fine. I heard games can be cancelled when the rain gets harsh.

Will definitely come back 🤙🏽🤙🏽

Just had my second successful hip replacement operation at Meirikal Tokyo Yamato Hospital in two years. The professionalism, level and quality of care was first rate, again. They were friendly and very patient with me, making communication in English very easy. I couldn’t be more pleased with the outcome.

I recently underwent kidney stone surgery at this hospital, and I couldn't be more impressed with the care I received. From the knowledgeable and compassionate medical staff to the state-of-the-art facilities, every aspect of my experience exceeded my expectations. The procedure went smoothly, and I felt well-cared for throughout my stay. I highly recommend this for anyone in need of kidney stone treatment.

Visited for a long-term urinary problem that I had struggled to get other health practicioners to take seriously. My Japanese is limited, so I was somewhat anxious at first, but this was by far the easiest and most reassuring hospital visit I have ever had.

On arriving at reception with a referral from my primary practicioner, the staff immediately flagged down a fluent English-speaking nurse who assisted me with the initial paperwork and ensuring I understood where to go and what was happening. My doctor was also excellent both in her English abilities and her professional manner, successfully diagnosing my problem within minutes of speaking with me and quickly confirming it with minimal testing.

All necessary examinations and procedures were carried out in the same visit, and at each stage all staff made my comfort and understanding their utmost priority. My visit finished with a follow-up appointment being arranged to ensure that my recovery was going smoothly. For any English-speaking visitors or residents in the Tokyo area seeking a friendly and capable urology hospital, I can confidently recommend this one as top-of-the-line.

ChIJiS65VwCNGGARnumoj3IeZQ8.name: SUI Bake & Cafe 神楽坂本店
ChIJiS65VwCNGGARnumoj3IeZQ8.address: Japan, 〒162-0812 Tokyo, Shinjuku City, Nishigokenchō, 11−１ 五軒町ビル

reviews.text: Met the owner and she was really nice. The pastries were the star. Each pastry I tasted was worth going back again. I recommend their egg tart for first timers

Official social media account: @sui.kagurazaka
Easiness of visit: ★★☆☆☆

《Run's Recommendations in 1 Minute!》
This cafe is located about a 3-4 minute walk from Edogawabashi Station. It offers takeout and dine-in options.
There are only five counter seats, so it's recommended for small groups and with limited luggage. More customers opt for takeout than dine-in.

Enjoy scones, egg tarts, and savory tarts with hot coffee or tea.

Customer demographics and impressions during visit
Gender ratio: 1:1
Age range: 20s-50s
Best time to visit: Solo, with friends, takeout
Visit time: Sunday 3:00 PM / No wait
Previous customers: 3
Accepted payment methods: Card, e-money, code payment, cash
Transportation: 6-minute walk from Edogawabashi Station

Order (Recommended)
"Matcha Cream Puff" (¥459)
"Egg Tart" (¥346)
"Milk Tea" (¥550)

˚₊⊹ ࣪˖˚*.⋆☽︎︎·̩͙*˚log˚₊⊹ ࣪˖˚*.⋆☽︎︎·̩͙*˚

This shop just opened in October 2025.
When I visited at 3:00 PM, there were two customers eating in and one customer ordering takeout.
There was only one pie left, made with Kagoshima black pork, but the other pie was still available.

When I dine in, I had the Matcha Cream Puff.
It was sprinkled with matcha powder on top, wrapped in paper and eaten like a hamburger.
The cream inside had a faint matcha flavor, but was not too sweet and delicious.

I think the tea was Assam? The plump mug was cute.

I also ordered the Egg Tart to go, which I had been interested in after reading the reviews.
This one was filled with soft custard and had a gentle sweetness.
The pie crust was moist and made a perfect gift.

Thank you for the meal!

The egg tarts are a must-buy! The pies and cream puffs are delicious, too. However, if I were to buy just one item as a souvenir, I would definitely choose the egg tarts! This shop looks set to become a new staple in the area! I wholeheartedly recommend it!

Honestly, I want to gatekeep this place but I've been a few times and the staff has always been so sweet, I feel like I need to leave a review :) Very cozy, very kind workers. The coffee is so good and rich! I always get the standard plate because the yogurt and granola are so good. Definitely worth coming here for a meal and to sit and relax!
To get there, you go in through the front lobby of the apartment building. Go to the elevators and up to the second floor. It'll be right there when you get out of the elevator :)

How have I lived in Itabashi for 4 months and just discovered this place? You feel like you are going to visit a friend in their apartment. It’s kinda like going to one of the hidden speak easy bars like JANAI (which you should try as well!). Fantastic vibe, good eclectic music playing from good quality speakers. Nice open space with tons of natural light coming through a wall of glass looking out on the neighborhood. I had a breakfast set with a hot latte and every ingredient was quality and perfectly prepared. So delicious! I will definitely be back…and back again!

We've been staying in Ikebukuro during our trip, and I decided to check out this place as the aesthetic looked good from pictures. So happy I did, as the latte I got was delicious!

The place can be a little tricky to locate as it is on the second floor of a residential-looking building, and signage is easy to miss. Great spot, so don't miss out!

Since moving to Tokyo earlier this year, I’ve made Tokyo Union Church my spiritual home. Initially, I was drawn in by its photos on Google Maps and its central location. But what truly convinced me to stay was the welcoming and genuinely open atmosphere.

Every service leaves me uplifted and inspired. The messages are always relevant, thoughtfully delivered, and offer fresh, practical insights that stay with me. I especially value that the church approaches the Bible with a balanced and thoughtful perspective—never extreme or dogmatic, but always inclusive and respectful of different viewpoints.

One unexpected delight has been the choir, whose level of talent and coordination genuinely astonished me; it feels almost like attending a professional concert each week.

It’s my kind of church.
A very warm and welcoming Church community which I am grateful to attend. A great place to get to know fellow brothers and sisters in Christ. Truly have nothing negative to say here 👏
I am glad that our family did attend the special Christmas service on Christmas Day!

ChIJ5W88Bb6rNTERS1bmOSwQuwQ.name: Vietnam Tuong Theatre
ChIJ5W88Bb6rNTERS1bmOSwQuwQ.address: 51A P. Đường Thành, Cửa Đông, Hoàn Kiếm, Hà Nội, Vietnam

reviews.text: An notable building strolling from Old Quarter to Train Street. The road traffic is crazy.

The Vietnam Tuong Theatre also known as the Hanoi Opera House has nothing to envy from  such theatres around the world. it has beautiful design, great decor and a true class act. I really enjoyed being there.

Sometimes going down the wrong street is just the beginning of something great. Jumped into a coffee shop for some respite from the heat and a Saigon Coffee. Watched the Theater from across the road and visited after a little second hand cigarette smoke. A great little theater and so a tip, you can pay outside for what you can afford. The Theater has some great displayed costumes, weapons and masks. I watched the Lune production Lang Toi (My Village) honestly the best show of seen this side of a Movie, Tv, or Theater Screen. So thats why the 5. the Timing show and location couldn't have been better.

ChIJUVegyWiLGGARtd0zbaQT2Ko.name: Tokyo Jogakkan Junior & Senior High School

ChIJVW7dj9GrNTERfnxk5lvzCB0.name: Galaxy Mipec Long Biên
ChIJVW7dj9GrNTERfnxk5lvzCB0.address: Tầng 6, Chung Cư Mipec Riverside, P. Long Biên 2, Ngọc Lâm, Bồ Đề, Hà Nội 100000, Vietnam

reviews.text: This is the best cinema in Long Bien. The popcorn is actually warm and fresh. The picture and sound is very good. Most importantly it's very clean and I haven't seen any insects here.
